title: capture-implement-diff misclassifies a staged-deletion planned file as untracked, gitignore guard aborts commit
description: MACHINERY (surfaced by flow-kwai run 99e27e442f7b66ce, friction fb23b4f033ea42508930c02311cd3517). capture_implement_diff (diff_extract.py:332) classifies planned files via _untracked_files (git ls-files). A 'cached-removal' staged deletion (git rm --cached, used to untrack a file while keeping the working copy) is absent from git ls-files, so the path reads as untracked-new. _gitignored (diff_extract.py:337-342) then matches it against a .gitignore rule added in the SAME change and raises _IgnoredPlannedFile: 'planned file(s) gitignored, cannot be committed: <path>' (exit 1), aborting the commit stage. But the diff IS committable: git diff HEAD emits the staged deletion + the .gitignore hunk cleanly; no intent-to-add is needed for a deletion. The guard's premise (untracked + gitignored = uncommittable newly-created file) is false for an intentional untrack-and-ignore. FIX: exempt staged deletions (git diff --cached --diff-filter=D --name-only, or git ls-files --deleted) from the untracked/intent-to-add candidate set before the _gitignored check. HOT: diff_extract.py is in triage._GUARD_FILES (content-ownership commit gate), so the fix needs the merge-time guard-property review. Repro: any /flow ticket whose plan untracks-and-gitignores a planned file (exactly flow-kwai: untrack .claude/settings.json). BLOCKS flow-kwai.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) Proceed. The fix is correct and safe to auto-ship despite being a hot/guard-file change. Which option: exempt staged deletions (git diff --cached --diff-filter=D --name-only) from the untracked candidate set in capture_implement_diff before the _gitignored check and intent-to-add; ls-files --deleted is the wrong query (opposite case). Why safe: blast radius is one function (_untracked_files has a single caller), the change can only un-break a previously-false abort (additive subtraction from a list), revert is trivial, and CI coverage is strong (existing capture tests + 3 new TDD regression tests, incl. preservation of the genuine gitignore-rejection guard). Independent reviewer scored 93% and ruled proceed on both axes. Hot proceed permitted under [evolve] adjudicate_hot; merge-time guard-property review remains the gate.

title: tmp paths use bare /tmp, blocked by sandbox write allowlist
description:
Flow reference files write scratch files to bare /tmp, which the Claude Code sandbox write allowlist denies (only $TMPDIR, /tmp/claude, /private/tmp/claude permitted). Commit stage dies before push with: 'operation not permitted: /tmp/flow-commit-<KEY>.txt exit=1'. Surfaced in transcript 4121956e (Jun 10 12:07); misdiagnosed at run time as a bitbucket network whitelist issue.

Fix: swap /tmp/ -> $TMPDIR/ in:
- plugins/flow/skills/flow/references/verb-init.md:17  (mktemp /tmp/flow-init-XXXXXX.json)
- plugins/flow/skills/flow/references/verb-spec.md:51  (PLAN=/tmp/flow-plan-$KEY.md)
- plugins/flow/skills/flow/references/stage-commit.md  (/tmp/flow-commit-<KEY>.txt, 5 refs)

Leave test mock fixtures (/tmp/x.png in test_beads_adapter.py, test_tracker.py) untouched - no real write. CLAUDE.md already mandates $TMPDIR.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: Already resolved at base by PR #227 (18cd9b7, 'fix: survive harness bash sandbox in bg runs'), merged before this bead was filed. Every line this ticket cites already uses ${TMPDIR:-/tmp} on origin/main: verb-init.md:17, verb-spec.md:51, stage-commit.md:69/71/92. Verified no bare-/tmp write site remains anywhere in flow scripts or docs at origin/main (only $CLAUDE_JOB_DIR/tmp in verb-evolve.md prose, which is legit). flow --auto branches off @default (origin/main), so the planned fix would land as a no-op empty PR (flow-749 drift-vs-base). Closing as duplicate / already-fixed rather than shipping an empty PR. The launcher checkout (HEAD) lags origin/main, which is why the bug was still visible at filing time.

title: version_remerge recover: exits 0 leaving merge uncommitted when stamp-replace fails ('no version line to replace')
description: Witnessed 2026-06-09 draining PR #190 (flow-hw1.5): branch stamped 0.27.61 after main moved to 0.27.60 (PR #191) -> mergeStateStatus DIRTY -> version_remerge.py recover --branch ... printed 'no version line to replace in .../plugin.json' and exited 0, leaving the worktree MID-MERGE: both version files UU (unresolved index stage) though their working-tree content was already the resolved 0.27.61, merge commit not created, nothing pushed. Protocol (stage-merge step 3 Option B) requires recover to push a new SHA on RC=0; an RC=0 that pushes nothing breaks the drain's 'RC 0 -> re-wait CI -> merge' contract silently. Likely cause: the auto-resolve replace regex does not match a conflicted (or already-stamped same-version) file, and the failure path returns success without git add/commit/push or aborting the merge. Fix: make that path either complete the resolution (add+commit+push) or abort the merge and exit 3 (non-version conflict path); never exit 0 mid-merge. Hand recovery used: git add both files, git commit --no-edit, git push. Repro fixture: branch with stamp commit X+1 while origin/main sits at X.

title: create_pr base branch: respect [create_pr] base config instead of hardcoded --base main
description: stage-create_pr.md step 1 hardcodes '--base main' and create_pr.py's --base default is 'main' with no config fallback. A workspace whose integration branch is not main (e.g. brinta-data-platform, default branch dev — first real forge-seam Bitbucket workspace, migrated 2026-06-09 for flow-2je step (a)) gets its PR opened against the wrong branch (or fails if main does not exist). Fix: create_pr.py reads optional [create_pr] base from workspace.toml (same pattern as _draft_config; CLI --base wins only when explicitly passed, else config, else 'main'), and stage-create_pr.md drops the literal '--base main' in favor of the config-resolved default. Add tests beside the existing create_pr tests. Seam: keep the --base flag (prose may still name it); run seam_check.py. Not hot (create_pr.py not in guard set). Until this merges + the vdsmon-flow marketplace updates, /flow runs on brinta-data-platform must not reach create_pr.

title: flow --auto: advisor adjudicates judgment forks instead of deferring; fold confidence into ship/block/defer verdict
description: Replace the --auto defer-and-exit on judgment forks with an advisor RULING (3-way proceed/block/defer verdict assessed independently of is_hot_change), and fold the flat 90% confidence cliff into that verdict. Flag-gated maintainer-only. HOT (approval/bootstrap seam). Unblocks the deferred queue (flow-doo/nr4/yga/594). Full design in design notes.
design:
## Problem

In `--auto`, the spec/plan gate (`references/verb-spec.md` step 5) defers a bead whenever the headless Plan subagent raises a clarifying question, scores < 90% confidence, or BAILs — and there is no maintainer-recorded decision. The launcher walked away, so there is nobody to ask; the bead drops to `deferred`/`blocked` and waits for a human triage→reopen→re-run-without-`--auto`. This parks every judgment fork even when the right call is obvious to a strong reviewer. Current live victims: flow-doo, flow-nr4, flow-yga, flow-594 (the deferred queue is mostly these).

Two coupled flaws:
1. **No autonomous adjudication.** A judgment fork (a design knob, an opt-in-vs-unconditional choice) is parked rather than resolved, even though the `advisor` second-mind is already wired in this same path (it scores confidence today).
2. **The 90% confidence gate is a flat cliff.** It is a proxy for "how bad if wrong," but ignores the two axes that actually decide that: blast-radius/reversibility and CI-verifiability. flow-nr4 deferred at 88% — two points under a round number, on a clean contained change.

## Mechanism (reuse, don't rebuild)

At the one defer point (verb-spec.md step 5, the "NOT decided → defer-and-exit" branch), instead of parking, escalate the fork to the `advisor` for a RULING, not just a score. The advisor writes the `DECISION:` comment a human triager would have written; everything downstream is unchanged — the existing decided-probe (`scripts/triage.py` `_DECISION_STEMS`) sees it, the run proceeds, and a relaunch is idempotent (no re-ask).

The advisor returns a **3-way ship verdict, assessed independently of `is_hot_change`**:

| verdict | action |
|---------|--------|
| `proceed` | write `DECISION: (advisor) <ruling>`, self-approve, ship (CI-gated) |
| `block`   | rulable but unsafe to auto-ship (broad blast radius / irreversible) → comment + `bd status blocked` for human merge |
| `defer`   | needs maintainer-only information, or advisor low-confidence → defer-and-exit (today's behavior, unchanged) |

### Why the 3-way matters — flow-doo is the trap

`is_hot_change` is purely file-path based (guard/lease/safety files). flow-doo is NOT a guard file → "not hot" → a naive "non-hot judgment fork → proceed" design would auto-ship it. But the bead says verbatim: "BLAST RADIUS: get() is called broadly... propose, not auto-apply." File-path hotness is not blast-radius risk. So the advisor must answer "safe to auto-ship?" SEPARATELY from "which option is right?". `is_hot_change` stays a hard floor (hot → never `proceed`), but is no longer the only gate.

Mapped to the live queue: flow-nr4, flow-yga → `proceed` (clean, contained); flow-doo → `block` (rule the opt-in param, but broad blast → human merges); flow-594 → `block` (hot + irreversible).

## Fold the confidence gate into the verdict (kill the cliff)

With advisor-adjudication added, a separate 90% hard cliff is redundant — the advisor IS the second mind. Scope this to the `--auto` path only (the interactive path's 90% gate is correct: it means "exhaust read-only artefacts, then present to the human"; the human is present).

- Below a low floor (~70%): defer immediately, WITHOUT burning an adjudication call (plan too shaky to even ask).
- At/above the floor: hand the advisor `{plan, confidence, blast-radius, reversibility, CI-coverage}` and let it return `proceed` / `block` / `defer`.
- 90% stops being magic: an 85% clean-reversible change can `proceed`; a 95% irreversible one can `block`.

## Guardrails (non-negotiable)

- **Advisor absent → defer, never self-judge.** The existing "general-purpose Agent fallback" is the same model tier grading its own plan — fine for scoring, NOT for a binding ship decision. No stronger mind present → fall back to defer.
- **Distinct stem `DECISION: (advisor)`** so `/flow triage` can surface advisor-minted rulings for optional maintainer review; otherwise they are indistinguishable from human decisions on every relaunch and the audit trail is purely passive. (`triage.py _DECISION_STEMS` / `verb-triage.md` need the new stem recognized.)
- **`is_hot_change` hard floor preserved** — hot files never `proceed`, regardless of advisor verdict.
- **Flag-gated, maintainer-only** (`[evolve] advisor_adjudicates`, default OFF in `workspace.toml`), mirroring `[evolve] auto_merge_hot`. User projects keep the human-decision keystone; only the maintainer self-target repo opts in.

## Files (HOT — touches the approval/bootstrap seam)

- `references/verb-spec.md` — rewrite the step-5 "NOT decided → defer-and-exit" branch into the floor-check → advisor-adjudicate → proceed/block/defer flow.
- `scripts/triage.py` — recognize the `DECISION: (advisor)` stem (extend `_DECISION_STEMS`); possibly a helper to mint the advisor decision comment.
- `references/verb-triage.md` — document the advisor-minted decision stem + how triage surfaces it.
- `workspace.toml` + the config reader — `[evolve] advisor_adjudicates` flag (default off).
- Version bump (`plugin.json` + `marketplace.json`, in sync) — auto-included by bootstrap for maintainer plugin-code tickets.

## Tests

- advisor `proceed` on a non-hot clean fork → self-approves, ships.
- advisor `block` on a non-hot broad-blast fork (flow-doo shape) → status `blocked`, no PR.
- `is_hot_change` true → never `proceed` even on advisor `proceed` (hard floor).
- advisor absent → defer (not self-judge).
- confidence < floor → immediate defer, no adjudication call.
- flag off → today's behavior exactly (defer-and-exit).
- relaunch reads `DECISION: (advisor)` as decided → no re-ask.

## Note

This is the dogfood case for the very autonomy it adds — run it through `/flow`, not a hand-edit. HOT, so it routes through human merge regardless of `auto_merge_hot`.

title: Post-merge #93: switch nightly to evolve audit + drain (atomic with marketplace update)
description: After PR #93 (L3 evolve sub-verb namespace) merges, do these in ONE sitting so the 03:17 nightly never no-ops: (1) advance local main to origin/main; (2) claude plugin marketplace update vdsmon-flow; (3) edit ~/.flow-evolve/nightly-evolve.sh line ~70 'claude --bg /flow evolve' -> 'evolve audit' and line ~81 'claude --bg /flow evolve --ship' -> 'evolve drain'. Then live-smoke the dispatch (the only L3 verification gates cannot do): /flow evolve -> sub-verb listing; /flow evolve launch --dry-run -> plan no action; /flow evolve drain --dry-run -> both plans no action. Hazard if non-atomic: nightly fires bare evolve (now lists, files nothing) + evolve --ship (now unknown sub-verb) = silent no-op night.
close_reason: Done: #93 merged, local main advanced to d5233b7, marketplace updated, nightly-evolve.sh switched to 'evolve audit' + 'evolve drain' (syntax-checked), dispatch live-smoked (bare evolve -> listing; launch/janitor backends produce plans).

title: drainer: /flow evolve --ship — local parallel backlog consumer
description:
FOUNDATION BUILT + PR #19 (v0.25.0). evolve_select.py + evolve_reap.py + verb §6. 33 tests, lint+seam+877 green. Both smoke-run live (parse clean). Keystone spike passed (detached claude --bg runs to completion).

DEFERRED / PRECONDITIONS before first unattended --ship:
1. End-to-end loop UNPROVEN: /flow <key> --auto -> detached draft PR -> reap -> merge never exercised (only pure logic + spawn primitive). First --ship MUST be attended: watch one gh pr ready->merge to main succeed before trusting unattended.
2. OPEN DESIGN Q: hot beads get auto-LAUNCHED in --auto (draft-only, no auto-merge). User scoped non-hot for auto-MERGE but never for auto-LAUNCH. Confirm intended (live smoke launches flow-aut.6 hot).
3. WATCH: --delete-branch may orphan the launched .claude/worktrees tree; watch worktree accumulation across --ship cycles, add cleanup if needed.

title: Expose ticket creation in tracker_cli.py
description: The tracker abstraction implements create() for both backends (tracker_jira.py, tracker_beads.py) but tracker_cli.py exposes no 'create' subcommand. No prose-seam path can author a ticket. The capability exists in the engine; the CLI seam is the gap.

title: Build /flow evolve foundation (Rung 2: Steps 1-3)
description: Turn the Rung-1 one-shot audit into real machinery per the approved plan (~/.claude/plans/but-wait-the-curious-puppy.md). Step 1: tracker_cli.py create subcommand (flow_beads_create wrapper; BEADS_DIR targeting; refuse non-Flow targets). Step 2: reflect sling-bead on the propose+record path, gated on maintainer mode + recall filter for MACHINERY entries. Step 3: /flow evolve cold-audit verb (router row + references/verb-evolve.md) with stable evidence-hash bead IDs + closed/rejected memory so it converges. Maintainer detection: committed marker in .flow/workspace.toml, NOT an env var. Defer cloud shipper (Steps 4-5) until Rung 3 PR quality proven — now proven (PRs #8-#11). Open wrinkles: committed-marker-vs-fork contradiction; verify machinery_edit targets dev repo vs installed copy.

title: Jira adapter write/mutation methods have zero test coverage (create, link, all setters, board_rank, attachments)
description:
EVIDENCE:
tracker_jira.py defines create (554), set_summary (580), set_description (588), set_priority (591), set_labels (594), set_assignee (597), link (649), board_rank (819), get/upload_attachments, set_components, set_custom_field. Verified: grep for `.create(`/`.link(`/`.set_summary(` in test_jira_adapter.py = 0; the 42 tests cover only reads/transitions/sprint/watcher/fix-versions/epic-link. The beads equivalents ARE tested (test_beads_adapter.py:333,353,365 call adapter.create). These methods build branching REST payloads (create: plain-vs-ADF summary, optional parent/labels/assignee at 563-577), so a payload-shape regression ships silently to live Jira.

VALUE / RATIONALE:
Jira write paths are how /flow mutates real tickets; an untested PUT/POST payload that Jira rejects or mis-applies corrupts tracker state for paying users, and the asymmetry means the cheaper backend (beads) is the verified one. High value, strong evidence — but many methods, so not 'small/isolated'; ranks below the one-liners. Touches only the test file.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/tests/test_jira_adapter.py
HOT FILE: False
AUDIT SOURCES: tests-coverage mining
AUDIT ID: jira-write-methods-untested

title: pending-mutations queue is built + tested but no adapter or stage ever enqueues; transient tracker mutations are silently lost
description:
EVIDENCE:
Verified: append_mutation has zero non-test, non-self callers (grep of append_mutation across *.py returns only its definition at pending_mutations.py:152, a docstring, and the __all__ export). Only the drain side is wired: sync.py:87 list_mutations, :101 compact. tracker_beads.py and tracker_jira.py contain zero 'pending' references. The commit-stage transient-failure branch (stage-commit.md:94-97) logs a warning and continues — it drops the mutation, never enqueues. sync.py:3 and verb-sync-baseline.md assert the file is 'written by adapter failure paths' — false for both adapters. This is the unaddressed core of the stale TODO at tracker_beads.py:19-23 ('TODO (phase 8): transient-failure paths should append to pending-mutations.jsonl ... until phase 8 ships'), which contradicts dev-history.md (phase 8d shipped /flow sync) — fold the stale-TODO finding in here, it is the breadcrumb for the same unwired enqueue.

VALUE / RATIONALE:
Highest value of the set: in unattended runs a network/auth blip on a tracker transition (or comment/link/edit) is dropped permanently and /flow sync can never reconcile it because nothing wrote it. The whole pending-mutations subsystem (module + CLI + drain + verb prose) is dead weight until a write path is wired in. BUT: blast-radius-safety pulls this down — wiring append_mutation into the commit/ticket transient-failure branches touches wired adapters (tracker_beads/jira) and stage-commit.md, so it is NOT a safe small ship. Rank high on value, flag the tradeoff. Touches wired handlers = hot.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/tracker_beads.py, plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/tracker_jira.py, plugins/flow/skills/flow/references/stage-commit.md, plugins/flow/skills/flow/references/stage-ticket.md, plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/pending_mutations.py
HOT FILE: True
AUDIT SOURCES: friction-history mining
AUDIT ID: pending-mutations-never-enqueued

title: Flow self-evolution: Rung 1 audit backlog
description:
Backlog from the Rung 1 cold self-audit of the flow repo (see plan: Flow self-evolution proactive nightly loop).

The premise holds: this cold self-audit produced a genuine, non-noise yield. The shippable-as-small-tickets head is real — ty-skips-hooks (one-token fix, every-session blast), inventory.md --labels/--set-labels (one-line, ironclad), git quotePath (concrete correctness bug, confirmed 0 quotePath calls), the missing-stage-docs fix+guard pair, and the pending-mutations dead-enqueue (highest value, though it touches wired adapters so not a safe-small ship). Below that sits a legitimate but low-stakes housekeeping tail (duplicated _parse_iso/_atomicio/Runner helpers, complexity hotspots, stale comments, perms-narrowing). All top claims were re-verified empirically (lint task omits hooks, MODULE.md has 0 heartbeat refs, both registry stage docs MISSING, append_mutation has no enqueue caller, set_labels uses --set-labels, 0 quotePath in diff_extract, jira write methods have 0 test refs). Net: self-evolution mining works; ship the high-evidence head, batch the tail.

Every child carries file:line / reproduced-command evidence. Label `evolve`. The small leaf head ships as draft PRs first (Rung 3).
close_reason: All Rung-1 audit children shipped (aut.1-20 merged/closed across sessions; final batch this drain). Epic complete.

title: ticket stage: claim tracker backend (open->in_progress) to prevent fleet double-pick
description: The ticket stage (references/stage-ticket.md, handler=inline) stamps .flow/tickets/<KEY>.md frontmatter status=in_progress but never transitions the tracker BACKEND. On beads the bd issue stays 'open' for the whole run, so 'bd ready' still lists the in-flight ticket as available and a parallel fleet agent could double-pick it. Fix: add a best-effort backend transition (open->in_progress) to the ticket stage, mirroring the stage-commit transition step (MCP-first, REST/tracker_cli fallback), tolerant of exit 3 (no transition available / already in_progress) and transient faults so it never fails the run. Surfaced on flow-7qm with the machinery lens ON (PROPOSE+RECORD).

title: retirement
description:
Child-5 (HOT) of epic flow-8by2. RE-SCOPED by the flow-8by2.1 spike (PR #306).

Retire the launch_ledger.py TTL blind-window logic FULLY. But DEMOTE the ~/.claude/jobs dir scan to a reconcile/orphan-detection fallback rather than deleting it: a run that crashes BEFORE its first ledger register is invisible to a pure ledger, yet visible today as a ~/.claude/jobs/<id>/ dir, so deleting the scan is a regression. Gate retirement on a property-equivalence proof, NOT corpus-green alone (CLAUDE.md robustness floor). GATED on children 2-4 landing.

The ledger token does NOT subsume version_remerge's git-resolution (version-only-conflict detection, next-from-main) — that intrinsic content arbitration stays; version_remerge is DEMOTED to fallback. Token must wrap the fetch+merge+push span, not just gh-merge.

Cutover MUST move each destructive act (reap gh-merge + bd-close; cleanup claude-stop + teardown) to re-check the ledger UNDER the fleet flock immediately before the irreversible step, else the worst race survives the rebuild.

Surface parked PRs that have NEW unresolved human review threads as the next actionable item in the queue. Builds on child-1 (human-thread detection). BLAST RADIUS: queue surfacing/drain selection. Run via /flow <key> at its own spec-plan accept gate.

title: revision execution: review_loop fix-cycle with human threads as the Major+ set
description: Parent epic: flow-kx17 (order 4/5). NET-NEW. The revision fix-cycle: human review threads become the Major+ set the review_loop drives on, plus a severity mapping (change-requested review -> Major+, plain comment -> configurable) and the 3-fix-cycle cap semantics. Per cycle: commit/push/reply/resolve-thread/CI-rewait. Builds on child-1 (thread normalize+resolve) and child-2 (revision-run lifecycle). BLAST RADIUS: review_loop stage execution + severity-mapping config. Run via /flow <key> at its own spec-plan accept gate.

title: /flow revise <ticket|pr> verb + SKILL.md routing row (HOT)
description: Parent epic: flow-kx17 (order 3/5). NET-NEW, HOT: edits SKILL.md routing row so is_hot_change is always true — ships via the adjudicated hot path (merge-time guard-property review), per the 'new /flow verb is hot change' precedent. Adds the /flow revise <ticket|pr> verb + its reference doc + routing row. BLAST RADIUS: SKILL.md (guard file) + a new references/verb-revise.md. Run via /flow <key> at its own spec-plan accept gate; the hot floor is code-enforced at bootstrap.

title: KEYSTONE: revision-run lifecycle seam (sub-run under same ticket, own lease)
description: Parent epic: flow-kx17 (order 2/5). NET-NEW, KEYSTONE (skeptic-binding: design FIRST after the forge flip child-1). A done run has a released lease, terminal state.json, possibly-reaped worktree. A revision must be a SUB-RUN keyed under the same ticket with its OWN lease, append-only ship events, NEVER mutating terminal state. Includes worktree locate-or-reseed from the PR branch. Resume path: dispatch next returns done:true at dispatch_stage.py:353 today; verb-recover.md scopes to stuck/failed/drift only. BLAST RADIUS: run-lifecycle core (lease, state.json, ship events, worktree seed) — the highest-stakes child. Run via /flow <key> at its own spec-plan accept gate.

Flip forge_github.py reviewThreads support from hard-off (forge_github.py:55 supported:False, :192-199 NotSupported) to ON: normalize human PR reviews into the existing ReviewThread shape + post-reply/resolve-thread. The stated 'nothing to test against' reason is dissolved by this use case. GraphQL resolveReviewThread needs the same transient-401 absorption the CI-wait probe got (#238). BLAST RADIUS: forge_github.py adapter only; existing ReviewThread shape is the contract. Run via /flow <key> at its own spec-plan accept gate.

title: flow_worktree.py create lacks an epic-type guard (spec/bootstrap chokepoint)
description:
WITNESS: `/flow flow-8by2 --auto` was launched on an EPIC bead. flow_worktree.py create refuses terminal (exit 6), hot (exit 2), dup-claim (exit 4) beads but has NO issue_type=='epic' refusal, so an epic CAN reach bootstrap and be cram-shipped as a single PR -- the 'auto-ship fragments of an unaccepted epic' ouroboros the design explicitly names (verb-evolve.md §epic). This run dispositioned correctly in prose (left the epic open + commented, no bootstrap), but the structural floor is missing.

GAP: evolve_select.py filters `issue_type != 'epic'` UNCONDITIONALLY (drain structurally never launches an epic), but the spec/bootstrap chokepoint (flow_worktree.py create) does not mirror that filter. A manual or misrouted `/flow <epic> --auto` is the unguarded path.

FIX (single PR): add an epic-type refusal to flow_worktree.py create at the bootstrap chokepoint (alongside _refuse_terminal_bead), new exit code, + matching SKILL.md/verb-spec.md prose for the --auto disposition (leave-open, no defer -- defer's loop-prevention is moot since drain already filters epics, and --status deferred would wrongly shelve a build-now epic and mutate maintainer accept/shelve state).

HOT: touches flow_worktree.py (guard file) + SKILL.md -> is_hot:true, adjudicate at spec gate.

REF: parent witness flow-8by2 (epic), evolve_select.py epic filter, verb-evolve.md §epic/§E expand.

title: evolve_select._hot_inflight bd list query has no --limit 0 (50-limit truncation, PR#299 class)
description: evolve_select.py _hot_inflight() runs 'bd list -l <label> --status <active> --json' WITHOUT --limit 0. bd's default 50-row limit + priority sort can truncate a launched hot key out of the hot-keys set under a large active backlog, so hot_inflight could read False and let a second hot launch (breaks >=1-hot-at-a-time). Same class as PR#299/flow-8zdy (bd-list-default-limit-50). Not load-bearing today (<=handful active evolve beads) so deferred from flow-xp5r to keep that hot auto-merging diff minimal. FIX: add '--limit','0' to the bd list call; strictly more conservative (only adds keys -> only over-serializes a hot, never under). BLAST RADIUS: evolve_select._hot_inflight + a test asserting the query carries --limit 0.

title: queue_select.partition budget ignores in-flight launched_pending/live (same shape as flow-xp5r)
description: queue_select.py:76 partition() has its own copy of the evolve_select budget bug fixed in flow-xp5r/PR#303: budget is computed without subtracting in-flight active sessions (launched_pending UNION live_runs) from concurrency, so the day-job queue drain over-launches in the launch->init window. queue_select.py:165-185 already builds inflight_keys = live|launched but passes it only for candidate-exclusion, not budget. FIX: mirror the evolve_select fix — add inflight_count=len(live_keys | launched_keys) to partition() and budget = min(cap - open_pr_count, max(0, concurrency - inflight_count)). BLAST RADIUS: queue_select.partition/select + a regression test. Concurrency safety -> hot.

title: stage-merge.md §1 gate call uses zsh-breaking ${VAR:+--flag} conditional expansion
description: references/stage-merge.md:62 invokes evolve_self_merge.py with ${EVAL_STATUS:+--eval-status "$EVAL_STATUS"}. zsh keeps that whole construct as ONE token, so argparse rejects it ('unrecognized arguments: --eval-status pass') and the merge §1 gate errors out on any PR that touched scripts/*.py (i.e. every hot engine self-merge, where EVAL_STATUS is non-empty). Worked around in the flow-qwf3 run by re-invoking with an args-array form. This is the exact class flow-i256/PR#287 fixed (zsh-word-split-conditional-expansion); the fix is to build an args array (ARGS=(...); [ -n "$EVAL_STATUS" ] && ARGS+=(--eval-status "$EVAL_STATUS"); python3 ... "${ARGS[@]}") instead of the :+ conditional. Hot: edits references/stage-merge.md (a /flow reference doc on the merge keystone hot path).
close_reason: Duplicate of PR#287 (1d1439c): EVAL_STATUS args-array fix already at origin/main; local checkout lagged 24 commits. No-op PR avoided. Real fix = advance marketplace checkout.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: Already fixed at origin/main — duplicate of PR#287 (commit 1d1439c, "fix: fix EVAL_STATUS conditional expansion quoting in stage-merge.md §1"). At origin/main, stage-merge.md:62 is the prescribed args-array form (_merge_args=(...); [ -n "$EVAL_STATUS" ] && _merge_args+=(--eval-status "$EVAL_STATUS")); the ${EVAL_STATUS:+...} construct is gone. Verified: `git log HEAD..origin/main -- references/stage-merge.md` => 1d1439c (#287); grep of ${VAR:+ across SKILL.md + all references/*.md at origin/main => zero hits. A worktree off @default would land an empty PR, so no bootstrap. ROOT CAUSE of the false sighting: the local main checkout (which the vdsmon-flow marketplace tracks) lags origin/main by 24 commits, so live /flow runs still load the stale prose with the broken :+ at the merge gate (this is what flow-qwf3 hit). The real remaining action is the operational maintainer step: advance local main -> origin/main, then `claude plugin marketplace update vdsmon-flow` — NOT a code PR. Closing as duplicate.

title: evolve_reap._file_main_red_p0 open-bead dedup scan misses --limit 0 (same footgun as flow-8zdy)
description:
evolve_reap._file_main_red_p0 (evolve_reap.py ~line 357) runs [
  {
    "id": "flow-xp5r",
    "title": "evolve_select budget ignores in-flight launched_pending/live runs: over-launches in the launch-\u003einit window",
    "description": "OBSERVED (drain run 2026-06-12): evolve_select.select() computes budget = min(cap - open_pr_count, concurrency) (evolve_select.py:101). inflight_keys only DROP in-flight beads from the candidate 'active' set (line 88, prevents re-launch); they are NOT subtracted from budget. In the launch-\u003einit window (runs launched but no PR open yet: live_runs registered but open_pr_count still low), the selector re-offers a full budget every turn, so action stays 'launch' continuously until open PRs reach cap. Concretely: 8 runs launched (launched_pending=8, live_runs=[], open_pr_count=0) and the very next turn the selector offered 7 MORE (skipped_in_flight correctly listed the 8, yet budget=min(10-0,8)=8). Following action=launch blindly would fan out ~15 concurrent backgrounded · \u001b[36m2326ae21\u001b[39m\u001b[2m (idle — send a prompt to start)\u001b[22m\n\u001b[2m  claude agents             list sessions\u001b[22m\n\u001b[2m  claude attach 2326ae21    open in this terminal\u001b[22m\n\u001b[2m  claude logs 2326ae21      show recent output\u001b[22m\n\u001b[2m  claude stop 2326ae21      stop this session\u001b[22m sessions (real sessions, not workflow subagents) before any backpressure exists. WORKAROUND used this drain: the orchestrator throttled manually, holding total in-flight at concurrency via effective = max(0, concurrency - |launched_pending UNION live_runs UNION open_pr_keys|), launching only that many per turn and D-waiting for a real completion to free a slot. VALUE: the only cross-turn throttle today is open-PR backpressure (cap), which builds minutes late; concurrency=8 is set below cap=10 precisely as the tighter simultaneity bound but is not actually enforced across the window. FIX (candidate): subtract in-flight (launched_pending UNION live_runs) from budget, or count live+pending against concurrency directly. BLAST RADIUS: evolve_select.select() budget calc + a regression test (launched_pending non-empty, open_pr=0 -\u003e budget shrinks). Concurrency safety is load-bearing -\u003e treat as guard-adjacent / hot. SECONDARY (same window, minor): a run that completes faster than a drain poll cycle (flow-26zg: plan-\u003ePR-\u003eself-merge-\u003eclosed inside ~1 poll) leaves a stale launch_ledger marker that never cleared at registration (cli_main removes only while a live lease/open PR is observed; both were already gone), so it lingered in launched_pending until the 1800s TTL, falsely occupying a slot.",

    "id": "flow-nu1w",
    "title": "Cognitive-yield driver: absorb deterministic inline-stage recipes into the engine (spike-gated)",
    "description": "WHY (witnessed bug-class; skeptic verdict SURVIVE NARROWLY -- spike-only authorized, retargeted): the prose-CLI seam-drift bug class exists only because orchestration is prose (a 698-line seam_check.py + three drift-gate extensions: flow-nr4, flow-yyn, flow-yga); prose is not self-applying (flow-bzz: a stage-doc fix activates next run); the deterministic inline-stage recipes (stage-commit.md 154 lines, stage-merge.md 212 lines, create_pr) are where the drift class lives. SKEPTIC CORRECTIONS (binding, they reshape the epic): (1) the original token-burn grounding was EXAGGERATED -- dispatch_stage already owns the plumbing and the skeleton sits in context once per session; the spike must MEASURE mechanical-orchestration token share over ~5 recent --auto transcripts and everything downstream is gated on it being material (\u003e15%) OR justified by the drift-surface rationale alone; (2) retarget from none-handler/advance plumbing (already code, near-zero win) to absorbing the deterministic inline-stage recipes; (3) frame as EXTENDING dispatch advance (a run-until-cognitive-yield co-routine over repeated CLI calls -- only the LLM session can spawn Agent/Skill tools, so the driver is necessarily cooperative, NOT a new flow_run.py inversion); (4) the structured handler-result contract must not delete the descriptor seam user-mode runs depend on; (5) every child edits SKILL.md/dispatch_stage.py -\u003e hot throughout, drains sequentially. Thesis note: prose-as-orchestrator is part of self-evolution, but machinery_edit covers scripts equally and code is corpus-evaluable where prose is not -- the tension resolves in favor of moving DETERMINISTIC recipes to code while cognitive-stage protocols stay markdown. DECOMPOSITION PREVIEW (lazy; expand on accept; children 2-4 gated on child 1):\n1. (NET-NEW) spike: classify stage-iterations in 5 recent --auto transcripts mechanical-vs-cognitive, measure orchestration token share, publish the gate verdict -- dedup epic:cognitive-yield-driver::child-1-burn-spike\n2. (NET-NEW, hot) dispatch advance run-until-cognitive-yield mode + structured result contract (descriptor seam preserved) -- dedup epic:cognitive-yield-driver::child-2-yield-mode\n3. (NET-NEW, hot) absorb the stage-commit recipe -- dedup epic:cognitive-yield-driver::child-3-commit-absorb\n4. (NET-NEW, hot) absorb stage-merge/create_pr recipes + prose shrink + seam_check/corpus extension -- dedup epic:cognitive-yield-driver::child-4-merge-absorb\nCONFIDENCE: 0.6.     "description": "WHY (witnessed, strongest friction backing of the pass; skeptic verdict SURVIVE 0.75): the coordination layer was built for one run per ticket launched by a human; reality is 5+ parallel bg runs from two drain loops. The architecture generates the bug class faster than point-patches retire it: live run reaped mid-CI-wait (flow-72d9/#231), takeover TOCTOU twice in different paths (flow-ocp/#211, flow-s85/#235), launch_ledger prune crashed on every call since introduction (flow-l48/#168), marker-TTL blind window (flow-d4s/#173), version-stamp collisions needing a whole recovery script (#220/#226/#196); 36 of 246 repo commits churn the six coordination files; SIX coordination fixes landed on 2026-06-10 alone. Liveness is currently INFERRED from a five-source eventually-consistent join (leases + launch markers + gh PRs + bd status + ~/.claude/jobs scans -- the last reverse-engineered, schema-less, and the flakiest). SKEPTIC CORRECTIONS (binding): scope is collapsing the three LIVENESS sources (lease + launch markers + jobs scan) into one flock-serialized .flow/fleet/ authority -- gh/bd remain as work-state reads (one inference site instead of five, not zero); positive deregistration keeps a heartbeat-staleness fallback or a crashed run blocks the drain forever; per the VISION robustness floor the lease/TOCTOU properties are replaced only by provably-equivalent ones and the retirement child needs that proof, not just corpus green; the Agent-Teams alternative is folded into the spike (expected to lose: cross-boot detached processes, stdlib-only headless reads, and the one platform-coupled source today is the flakiest -- evidence against deeper coupling); success metric = coordination-fix commit share + reap incidents post-cutover. BLAST RADIUS: lease.py (guard), launch_ledger.py, evolve_select/drain/reap/session_cleanup, queue_select/drain, verb-evolve.md/verb-queue.md, harness corpus. Hot throughout; children serialize under one-hot isolation. DECOMPOSITION PREVIEW (lazy; expand on accept):\n1. (NET-NEW, hot) readers cutover: selectors/reap/cleanup read the ledger; reap requires positive dereg with staleness fallback -- dedup epic:fleet-coordination-authority::child-3-readers-cutover\n4.     "description": "WHY (witnessed workflow; skeptic verdict SURVIVE 0.80, all cites verified): maintainer leaves change-request comments on a flow-delivered PR -\u003e every revision round is manual today. Pipeline terminal is 'CI green + no unresolved Major+ BOT threads' (stage-review_loop.md p3); human review happens strictly after; GitHub review-threads capability is hard-off (forge_github.py:55 supported:False, :192-199 NotSupported -- its stated reason 'nothing to test against' is dissolved by this use case); no resume path for a done run (dispatch next returns done:true at dispatch_stage.py:353; verb-recover.md:3 scopes to stuck/failed/drift only). VISION: 'you spec the work and review the PR; the machine owns everything in between' -- the revision round triggered by that review is machine work with no track.     "id": "flow-ufvu",
    "title": "Temporal memory: supersession + selection pressure on knowledge.jsonl (stale lessons must die)",
    "description": "WHY (witnessed + web-grounded; skeptic verdict SURVIVE 0.8): knowledge.jsonl is append-only with NO supersede/retire mechanism (memory_append.py:18 'NEVER rewritten'); recall.py rank() is pure BM25 with ts only as tiebreak -- a stale term-rich DECISION outranks its successor. Live contradictions coexist NOW: flow-8we (06-04, 'version bumps PATCH-only never minor', line 44) vs flow-mse (06-10, 'MINOR on feat', line 187); flow-014 (bootstrap auto-includes version files, PR#117, line 91) vs flow-6gx.4 (removed entirely, PR#186, line 153). 244 entries in one month; the auto-memory MEMORY.md already hand-curates with OBSOLETE/RETIRED annotations -- the need is being paid manually. Web: Zep arXiv 2501.13956 / Graphiti validity-window facts; Mem0 reports large gains from consolidation+forgetting. VISION: memory is one of the two compounding loops; superseded DECISIONs fed into spec/recall poison it.     "id": "flow-sdkk",
    "title": "In-flight hotness: OR observed-diff-derived hotness into the run's own merge adjudication (additive, completes flow-7f1/flow-bgh)",
    "description": "PROPOSAL (judgment lane; the salvaged narrow ticket from a refuted planned-files-to-provenance epic -- the epic was refuted as circular: ownership-by-provenance degenerates the commit gate to always-pass; the commit gate KEEPS blocking on planned_files). The already-shipped pattern: evolve_self_merge (flow-bgh/#174) and evolve_reap (flow-7f1/#172) re-derive hotness from the real PR diff because the planned set under-declares. The remaining in-flight consumer still trusting planned_files alone is the run's own merge-stage adjudication path; OR the observed diff (diff vs base at merge time) into is_hot there too, strictly additive (observed can only RAISE hotness, never lower it).     "description": "## The gap (web-cited + a structural blind spot in the producer set)\n\nflow's friction feedstock is flow_friction.py — an append-only log HAND-WRITTEN by the do-loop prose, IN-FLIGHT, only when a step notices it worked around a snag (SKILL.md \"Friction logging (in-flight)\"). Producer A (the reflect sling) and Producer B (evolve audit, a cold scan over SOURCE CODE) both consume that log or read code. NEITHER mines completed run TRANSCRIPTS. A failure mode the prose never thought to log — a silent retry, a stage that took three attempts, a recurring drift the agent ate without writing an entry, a stall gap — is invisible to the loop. The feedstock is self-REPORTED, never self-EVIDENT.\n\n## The field moved here (the grounding)\n\nMining raw execution traces post-hoc is now a recognized discovery surface:\n- Self-Harness (arXiv 2606.09498) \"Weakness Mining\": reads execution traces, clusters failed runs by verifier-grounded failure signatures (terminal cause + agent-behavior contribution + reusable mechanism), feeding the harness-proposal stage.\n- Addy Osmani, Agent Harness Engineering: names the frontier directly — \"agents that analyze their own traces to identify and fix harness-level failure modes.\"\n- LangChain, The Anatomy of an Agent Harness: agentic judges inspect production traces, identify emerging failure modes, mint reusable labels.\n\n## VISION anchor\n\n\"The worker at the station sees what I can't\" — the transcript IS the worker's record, and flow currently only sees the friction the worker remembered to write down. A trace miner makes lived friction self-evident rather than self-reported, deepening Producer A's feedstock. Maintainer-gated, self-target only (mines flow's OWN dogfood run transcripts under ~/.claude, never user telemetry — not a platform). Produces deduped PROPOSALS through the existing seam; never auto-implements. A net-new producer at a DISCOVERY altitude flow lacks — distinct from the measurement epics, which grade edits flow already knows it made.\n\n## Distinct from existing producers\n\n- reflect (Producer A) = in-flight, self-reported friction during a run.\n- evolve audit (Producer B) = cold static scan of source code.\n- evolve propose / epic = generative judgment + theme-scale.\n- THIS = post-hoc mining of completed-run TRANSCRIPTS for failure modes none of the above recorded. Net-new feedstock, not a re-run of an existing scan.\n\n## Blast radius\n\nNew read-only producer script + a reflect/evolve entry point. Reads transcript JSONL (filesystem-scan only — NEVER `claude agents --json`, which blocks on a TTY per the known gotcha) + the existing friction log, files deduped evolve beads through the existing flow_beads_create.py + evid:\u003cfingerprint\u003e seam, so the existing drain consumer picks them up with zero new consumer code. (NET-NEW) File mined signatures as deduped evolve beads through flow_beads_create.py + the evid: fingerprint, so the existing drain consumer picks them up. dedup epic:transcript-trace-mining::child-3-file.\n4. (NET-NEW) Wire as a scheduled producer: add a trace-mining pass alongside evolve audit on the nightly cadence (loop-engineering.md), so transcript mining runs unattended. dedup epic:transcript-trace-mining::child-4-schedule.\n\n## Grounding cited\n\nWeb cites (Self-Harness Weakness Mining arXiv 2606.09498; Addy Osmani trace-analysis frontier; LangChain agentic trace judges) + reproduced witness (flow_friction.py is hand-logged in-flight; no producer reads run transcripts; friction.jsonl shows recurring RECONCILE classes the in-flight logger captured only partially).\n\n## Recommended default: needs-discussion (a net-new producer adds a standing surface; weigh against keeping the producer set lean).
