To unstick: expand the epic (/flow evolve epic, or create child-1), run the spike, decide on its numbers, then drain the children. [defer-reason: open-question]

title: Close the post-merge loop: immutable tier/version stamps, revert watcher, main-health gate
description:
WHY (witnessed; skeptic verdict SURVIVE ~0.70 NARROWED from 5 children to 3): the loop's learning ends at merge. metric.py revert-rate (:599-705) is a pure readout -- nothing consumes it; detection is tracker-only (a git revert on main with no bead reopen is invisible); ship events carry NO tier/model field (verified over all 18 events) while the tier:trivial label is MUTABLE (un-stamping destroys history -- the skeptic's correction: correlation is possible via label-join today but the record is not immutable, so child 1 is hardening); VISION:82 conditions the move-fast license on 'bad PR instantly correlated to its tier, reverted, the pattern un-stamped' and that actuation is a manual prose recipe (verb-evolve.md:237) with no watcher; auto_merge_hot is LIVE and 8 tier:trivial downshifts have shipped -- the license is being spent now with the correlation half manual. No main-branch health check anywhere: two concurrently-green PRs that semantically conflict land on main untested. SKEPTIC CORRECTIONS (binding): DROPPED revert->feedstock auto-filing (zero reverts ever -- defer until n_reverts>=1; a readout line in the watcher suffices) and the metric panel (ml7 territory). Child 3 is a per-drain-turn gate, not a standing watcher (no daemon to host one). Child 1 folds in the plugin-version stamp (prose-attribution salvage from a refuted prose-optimizer epic: stamping the shipping plugin version onto ship events + friction entries lets recall --metric correlate a prose PR with subsequent friction-per-run -- measurement without the optimizer). DECOMPOSITION PREVIEW (lazy; expand on accept):
1. (NET-NEW) stamp tier + plugin version into ship events and friction entries (forward-only, immutable, ml7.1 pattern) -- dedup epic:merged-outcome-feedback::child-1-ship-event-stamp
2. (NET-NEW) revert watcher: join ship events to tracker reopens AND git log --grep=revert on main; durable revert events + readout -- dedup epic:merged-outcome-feedback::child-2-revert-watcher
3. (NET-NEW, hot) per-drain-turn main-CI health gate: red main pauses auto-merge + files a P0 bead -- dedup epic:merged-outcome-feedback::child-3-main-health-gate
CONFIDENCE: 0.70. RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: build-now (child 1 is forward-only: every day of delay is permanent data loss).
close_reason: EPIC COMPLETE: all 3 children shipped — .1 tier/version stamps (PR#250), .2 revert watcher (PR#274), .3 main-CI health gate (PR#275). Post-merge loop closed: ship events carry immutable tier+version, reverts detected from both tracker and git main history, red main pauses all auto-merge lanes + files P0.

title: Fleet coordination authority: positive registration/heartbeat/deregistration ledger for the run fleet
description:
WHY (witnessed, strongest friction backing of the pass; skeptic verdict SURVIVE 0.75): the coordination layer was built for one run per ticket launched by a human; reality is 5+ parallel bg runs from two drain loops. The architecture generates the bug class faster than point-patches retire it: live run reaped mid-CI-wait (flow-72d9/#231), takeover TOCTOU twice in different paths (flow-ocp/#211, flow-s85/#235), launch_ledger prune crashed on every call since introduction (flow-l48/#168), marker-TTL blind window (flow-d4s/#173), version-stamp collisions needing a whole recovery script (#220/#226/#196); 36 of 246 repo commits churn the six coordination files; SIX coordination fixes landed on 2026-06-10 alone. Liveness is currently INFERRED from a five-source eventually-consistent join (leases + launch markers + gh PRs + bd status + ~/.claude/jobs scans -- the last reverse-engineered, schema-less, and the flakiest). SKEPTIC CORRECTIONS (binding): scope is collapsing the three LIVENESS sources (lease + launch markers + jobs scan) into one flock-serialized .flow/fleet/ authority -- gh/bd remain as work-state reads (one inference site instead of five, not zero); positive deregistration keeps a heartbeat-staleness fallback or a crashed run blocks the drain forever; per the VISION robustness floor the lease/TOCTOU properties are replaced only by provably-equivalent ones and the retirement child needs that proof, not just corpus green; the Agent-Teams alternative is folded into the spike (expected to lose: cross-boot detached processes, stdlib-only headless reads, and the one platform-coupled source today is the flakiest -- evidence against deeper coupling); success metric = coordination-fix commit share + reap incidents post-cutover. BLAST RADIUS: lease.py (guard), launch_ledger.py, evolve_select/drain/reap/session_cleanup, queue_select/drain, verb-evolve.md/verb-queue.md, harness corpus. Hot throughout; children serialize under one-hot isolation. DECOMPOSITION PREVIEW (lazy; expand on accept):
1. (NET-NEW) spike: race-matrix inventory of every coordination read/write + Agent-Teams comparison memo -- dedup epic:fleet-coordination-authority::child-1-race-matrix-spike
2. (NET-NEW, hot) fleet ledger: .flow/fleet/ flock-serialized registration+heartbeat+deregistration, subsuming launch markers -- dedup epic:fleet-coordination-authority::child-2-fleet-ledger
3. (NET-NEW, hot) readers cutover: selectors/reap/cleanup read the ledger; reap requires positive dereg with staleness fallback -- dedup epic:fleet-coordination-authority::child-3-readers-cutover
4. (NET-NEW) merge-serialization token in the ledger; version_remerge demoted to fallback -- dedup epic:fleet-coordination-authority::child-4-merge-token
5. (NET-NEW, hot) retire superseded machinery (TTL blind-window logic, jobs-dir scan) after property-equivalence proof + corpus eval -- dedup epic:fleet-coordination-authority::child-5-retirement
CONFIDENCE: 0.75. RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: build-now, spike first (children 2+ gated on the spike's race matrix).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: flow --auto note (not a defer, not a decision): this bead is an EPIC (a container), not a single-PR unit, so an autonomous spec run builds nothing here by design — auto-shipping fragments of an unaccepted epic is the ouroboros the producer path guards against (verb-evolve.md §epic). Left OPEN and untouched (no defer/block): drain already filters epics structurally, so deferring buys nothing and would wrongly shelve a build-now epic. Path forward (maintainer §E): accept the epic, run the expand step to materialize the 5 preview children as proposal beads, then run each child via `/flow <child-key>` at its own spec-plan accept gate. Filed follow-up for the missing epic-type guard at the bootstrap chokepoint.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: MAINTAINER DECISION 2026-06-13: child-1 race-matrix spike done (PR #306, scripts/fleet-race-matrix.md). Verdict GO (staged), conf 0.78: ~13 distinct TOCTOU races across 6 join sites / 5 sources justify one flock ledger; build beats adopt-harness-Agent-Teams (detached stdlib subprocess can't call agent-namespace tools; adopt fails headless + cross-reboot). Two non-negotiables bind the children: (1) heartbeat-staleness fallback (pure positive-dereg blocks the drain forever on a crash); (2) property-equivalence proof before any retirement. Children 2-5 proceed in order after the spike PR merges; hot ones (2,3,5) serialize under one-hot isolation, human-merged.

title: /flow revise: post-delivery human-review-feedback ingestion (the missing second half of 'review the PR')
description:
WHY (witnessed workflow; skeptic verdict SURVIVE 0.80, all cites verified): maintainer leaves change-request comments on a flow-delivered PR -> every revision round is manual today. Pipeline terminal is 'CI green + no unresolved Major+ BOT threads' (stage-review_loop.md p3); human review happens strictly after; GitHub review-threads capability is hard-off (forge_github.py:55 supported:False, :192-199 NotSupported -- its stated reason 'nothing to test against' is dissolved by this use case); no resume path for a done run (dispatch next returns done:true at dispatch_stage.py:353; verb-recover.md:3 scopes to stuck/failed/drift only). VISION: 'you spec the work and review the PR; the machine owns everything in between' -- the revision round triggered by that review is machine work with no track. Day-job leverage: queue drain parks PRs for the maintainer; post-review is fully manual there too. SKEPTIC CORRECTIONS (binding): the run-lifecycle seam is the KEYSTONE and is designed FIRST after the forge flip (a done run has a released lease, terminal state.json, possibly-reaped worktree -- revision must be a sub-run keyed under the same ticket with its OWN lease, append-only ship events, never mutating terminal state); human comments need an explicit severity mapping (change-requested review -> Major+, plain comment -> configurable) + decide the 3-fix-cycle cap semantics; the verb routing edit is SKILL.md -> always hot, ships via the adjudicated hot path; GraphQL resolveReviewThread needs the same transient-401 absorption the CI-wait probe got (#238). DECOMPOSITION PREVIEW (lazy; expand on accept; order 1 -> 2 -> 3 -> 4 -> 5):
1. (NET-NEW) forge_github reviewThreads ON: normalize human reviews into the existing ReviewThread shape + post-reply/resolve-thread, 401-absorbed -- dedup epic:human-review-revise::child-1-review-threads-on
2. (NET-NEW, KEYSTONE) revision-run lifecycle seam: sub-run under the same ticket (own lease, append-only ship events, terminal state untouched), incl. worktree locate-or-reseed from the PR branch -- dedup epic:human-review-revise::child-2-revision-lifecycle
3. (NET-NEW, hot) /flow revise <ticket|pr> verb + SKILL.md routing row -- dedup epic:human-review-revise::child-3-revise-verb
4. (NET-NEW) revision execution: review_loop fix-cycle with human threads as the Major+ set, severity mapping, commit/push/reply/resolve/CI-rewait -- dedup epic:human-review-revise::child-4-revision-execution
5. (NET-NEW) queue surfacing: parked PRs with new unresolved human reviews flagged as next actionable -- dedup epic:human-review-revise::child-5-queue-surfacing
CONFIDENCE: 0.8. RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: build-now (strongest capability candidate; every multi-round PR is a live witness).

title: Temporal memory: supersession + selection pressure on knowledge.jsonl (stale lessons must die)
description:
WHY (witnessed + web-grounded; skeptic verdict SURVIVE 0.8): knowledge.jsonl is append-only with NO supersede/retire mechanism (memory_append.py:18 'NEVER rewritten'); recall.py rank() is pure BM25 with ts only as tiebreak -- a stale term-rich DECISION outranks its successor. Live contradictions coexist NOW: flow-8we (06-04, 'version bumps PATCH-only never minor', line 44) vs flow-mse (06-10, 'MINOR on feat', line 187); flow-014 (bootstrap auto-includes version files, PR#117, line 91) vs flow-6gx.4 (removed entirely, PR#186, line 153). 244 entries in one month; the auto-memory MEMORY.md already hand-curates with OBSOLETE/RETIRED annotations -- the need is being paid manually. Web: Zep arXiv 2501.13956 / Graphiti validity-window facts; Mem0 reports large gains from consolidation+forgetting. VISION: memory is one of the two compounding loops; superseded DECISIONs fed into spec/recall poison it. SKEPTIC CORRECTIONS (binding): supersession must be ENTRY-ID-TARGETED, never topic-matched (flow-6gx.4 retained version_remerge as backstop -- a topic sweep would have hidden live knowledge); retro sweep is propose-only with human confirm; recency decay is cut or type-gated (FACTs are evergreen); the filter applies in recall.py AND the SessionStart recall hook path; tombstones-as-new-records preserve append-only and make wrong supersession recoverable. BLAST RADIUS: memory_append.py, recall.py, stage-reflect.md prose, hooks/session-start recall path, one new sweep producer. No guard files. DECOMPOSITION PREVIEW (lazy; expand on accept):
1. (NET-NEW) supersession schema (--supersedes <entry-id>, tombstone records) + recall/hook filter -- dedup epic:temporal-memory-supersession::child-1-supersede-schema
2. (NET-NEW) reflect-stage wiring: a run whose merged change disproves a recalled entry writes the id-targeted supersession -- dedup epic:temporal-memory-supersession::child-2-reflect-wiring
3. (NET-NEW) retro-curation sweep over the existing 244-entry stock, propose-only -- dedup epic:temporal-memory-supersession::child-3-retro-sweep
4. (NET-NEW, optional) type-gated recency handling in rank() -- dedup epic:temporal-memory-supersession::child-4-recency-decay
5. (NET-NEW, optional) corpus-health metric (live vs superseded counts) -- dedup epic:temporal-memory-supersession::child-5-corpus-health
CONFIDENCE: 0.8. RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: build-now (children 1-3 are the load-bearing core).

title: ml7 child-4: recall --metric trend roll-up printing all four measures over the window
description: The last unbuilt child of in-progress epic flow-ml7 (its preview gates child-4 on 'children 1-3 capture real data' -- PRECONDITION VERIFIED 2026-06-10 from repo root: tickets-per-week shipped=18 / via_flow=4, time-to-pr n_measured=4 median 0.15h, revert-rate 0/17, friction-per-run 1.43 over 46 runs). Build: one metric.py subcommand 'trend' rolling up all four measures over the window (table + --json), forwarded via recall --metric (generic forwarder, getattr-gate any new checkpoint arg per memory). Closes flow-ml7 on merge; also arms flow-oe9's stated revisit condition ('when recall --metric trend has real windows'). GUARD: depend on / coordinate with scripts/metric.py::cwd-silent-empty (the cwd-sensitivity bug) so the roll-up cannot silently read an empty workspace. BLAST RADIUS: metric.py + verb-recall.md + tests/test_metric.py. Non-hot.

Trend runner must dispatch-return in cli_main BEFORE the getattr(args,'checkpoint',False) block like the four existing runners; recall.py passthrough needs no edit. title: In-flight hotness: OR observed-diff-derived hotness into the run's own merge adjudication (additive, completes flow-7f1/flow-bgh)
description: PROPOSAL (judgment lane; the salvaged narrow ticket from a refuted planned-files-to-provenance epic -- the epic was refuted as circular: ownership-by-provenance degenerates the commit gate to always-pass; the commit gate KEEPS blocking on planned_files). The already-shipped pattern: evolve_self_merge (flow-bgh/#174) and evolve_reap (flow-7f1/#172) re-derive hotness from the real PR diff because the planned set under-declares. The remaining in-flight consumer still trusting planned_files alone is the run's own merge-stage adjudication path; OR the observed diff (diff vs base at merge time) into is_hot there too, strictly additive (observed can only RAISE hotness, never lower it). CONFIDENCE 0.55. RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: needs-discussion (marginal -- merge-time guards may already cover every real path; verify against stage-merge.md section 2 before building; close as wontfix if redundant). BLAST RADIUS: triage.py is_hot_change callers + stage-merge prose; guard-adjacent -> likely hot.

title: code_review fan-out: N lens-scoped reviewers + synthesis, tier-scaled, panel_size default 1 (gated on the review metric)
description: PROPOSAL (judgment lane; skeptic-corrected from epic to single PR, gated on scripts/metric.py::review-findings-metric). Fan out the code_review stage to N lens-scoped subagents (correctness / safety-property / tests) spawned in one message, synthesized in stage prose (agreement>=2 blocking, singleton advisory) -- NO new engine script; use the existing handler seam / stage-prose Agent fan-out. [code_review] panel_size workspace key, default 1 (today's behavior); tier:trivial stays 1, hot gets the panel. Skeptic findings honored: the merge stage ALREADY has an independent refuter (stage-merge.md:65-67) -- do not duplicate it; build only the code_review half; ship AFTER the review metric exists and only if it shows single-self-review missing real findings. CONFIDENCE 0.7 in this narrowed form. RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: build after the metric. BLAST RADIUS: stage-code_review.md prose + workspace.toml key + validate_workspace; non-hot (stage doc is not a guard file).
close_reason: TRIAGE-DECISION 2026-06-13: wontfix, paired with flow-0nhg. Bead's own RECOMMENDED DEFAULT gates it on a review metric that is now wontfix (flow-0nhg), so the blocker is permanent. Review-panel fan-out is the low-complexity speculative expansion xqt counsels bounding. Revive only if a lean review-effectiveness metric is built and shows single-self-review missing real findings.

title: Review-quality metric first: findings-per-PR + post-merge revert correlation by reviewer count/tier (gates any panel fan-out)
description: PROPOSAL (judgment lane; skeptic-corrected remnant of a refuted review-panel epic). The code_review stage is an inline SELF-review by the same context that wrote the code (stage-code_review.md concedes the bias as acceptable-for-personal-mode); the merge hot-guard check is one subagent one pass. Before buying N-reviewer fan-out, build the measure: extend metric.py with a review-effectiveness readout -- findings-per-PR (mined from code_review .out reports), post-merge revert/regression correlation, split by tier and (once it exists) panel size. VISION bar: better must be measurable; the open question N same-model reviewers may just vote the same blind spot is exactly what this metric answers before the fan-out ships. CONFIDENCE 0.7. RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: build. BLAST RADIUS: metric.py + verb-recall.md + tests, non-hot. Sequencing: this bead FIRST; the panel fan-out proposal (stage-code_review.md::panel-fanout) is gated on what this shows.
close_reason: TRIAGE-DECISION 2026-06-13: wontfix. findings-per-PR data source does not exist (stage-code_review.md: 'No file outputs'; no tier/panel/.out concept). Gates a refuted review-panel epic; closed per xqt bound-the-machinery verdict (machinery belongs on high-complexity/hot work, not speculative review fan-out).

title: ops deadman: scheduled evolve loop writes a durable run-record; SessionStart hook surfaces staleness
description: EVIDENCE (verified 2026-06-10): ops/nightly-evolve.sh.template + ops/weekly-epic.sh.template only tee to ~/.flow-evolve/logs/<ts>.log -- no run-record, no heartbeat, no staleness surface (grep heartbeat/deadman/stale in ops/ empty). The failure mode is witnessed, not hypothesized: the nightly template's own PATH comment (lines 26-29) is the fossil of a real silent death (launchd bare-PATH -> claude command not found, masked by by-hand testing); zombie --bg --auto runs were a second witnessed class. A dead nightly loop is currently discovered only by noticing PRs stopped appearing. Skeptic-adjudicated task-sized (not epic). FIX (one PR): (1) ~5 lines per template appending start/end/outcome to a durable run-record file; (2) a staleness function + render block in plugins/flow/hooks/session-start.py (fires every SessionStart, walks to any .flow root; absence of the record file naturally gates the warning to machines where the schedule is armed) warning 'nightly loop stale >36h / weekly >8d'; (3) note the manual redeploy step in the PR (templates are copy-deployed to ~/.flow-evolve/, the fix is inert on the live machine until redeployed). BLAST RADIUS: ops templates + hooks/session-start.py + hook tests. Non-hot, additive observability. Does not resurrect flow-qp7's per-run heartbeat (different object: per-schedule-fire).

title: metric.py silently returns all-zeros when invoked from the wrong cwd (workspace-root resolution finds empty workspace, exit 0)
description: EVIDENCE (reproduced live 2026-06-10): running recall --metric / metric.py from inside plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts resolves an empty workspace and returns all-zero metrics with exit 0; the same invocation from the repo root returns real data (tickets-per-week shipped=18, time-to-pr n=4, revert 0/17, friction 1.43/46 runs). This nearly produced a false 'no data accumulated' conclusion during an epic-lens analysis -- an unguarded consumer (the future trend roll-up, a scheduled producer, or the flow-oe9 revisit check) would mis-read loop health the same way. FIX: when workspace resolution finds no .flow (or an empty events store) fail loud (non-zero exit + stderr naming the resolved root) or at minimum stamp the resolved workspace root into the JSON output so consumers can sanity-check. Match whatever convention validate_workspace uses for not-a-workspace. BLAST RADIUS: metric.py + tests; possibly recall.py forwarding (getattr-gate any new arg per the flow-nn4 memory). Non-hot.

title: seam_check v2: gate the descriptor stdout-JSON keys and role-string literals the do-loop prose parses
description: EVIDENCE (verified 2026-06-10 @ 8fe8a86): seam_check validates only argparse flags/subcommands (seam_check.py:36-38, validate() :283) + MODULE.md/registry gates. But SKILL.md:111-114 tells the agent to parse ~16 stdout-JSON keys (done, blocked_by, reason, stage, handler_type, head_sha, ticket_dir, output_path, roles, reference_doc, subagent_type, skill_name, skill_args, reconciled_drift, finished, holder, run_id) -- all currently present in dispatch_stage.py (:59-70,:159-217,:346-379,:507), zero gate. A key rename passes seam_check + unit tests and breaks every run at SKILL.md step 4b. Sibling ungated literal: stage-registry.toml:75 roles=[records_diff_baseline] whose ONLY reader is SKILL.md:116 prose; renaming it silently disables the implement diff-baseline hook that the commit stage's git apply depends on. FIX: extend seam_check with (1) a descriptor-key gate -- extract the emitted-key set from dispatch_stage (static dict literals or a declared constant) and check prose-cited keys against it; (2) a role-literal gate -- prose role strings must exist in stage-registry roles arrays. Same one-directional missing-only philosophy as the surface-cell gate (flow-yyn). Out of scope (note for later, do not build now): exit-code cites, embedded .flow paths, PR_URL= token. BLAST RADIUS: seam_check.py + tests; possibly a small declared-constant refactor in dispatch_stage.py (then hot). High value: this is the same drift class seam_check was built for, one layer deeper.
close_reason: merged via [EXP ov] PR #267 (wave-5 winner, 10/10, version 0.38.0). seam_check v2 descriptor+role gates live

title: SKILL.md at 195/200 line ceiling: extract the PR-link presentation block to verb-do.md
description: EVIDENCE (verified 2026-06-10 @ 8fe8a86): SKILL.md = 195 lines vs the ~200 invariant (CLAUDE.md 'SKILL.md stays thin'). Next inline addition trips it -- and verb additions are already queued in the backlog (e.g. a future /flow revise). Move candidates, in order: (a) the PR-link presentation block SKILL.md:188-195 (rendering rules, --- rule example, caveat placement) is pure presentation, not hot-path orchestration -- belongs in verb-do.md next to the notification protocol already there, leaving a one-line pointer; (b) optionally the multi-positional-keys arbitration paragraph SKILL.md:41 -> verb-spec.md with a one-line router pointer. VALUE: restores headroom before it forces a rushed extraction inside an unrelated hot PR. BLAST RADIUS: SKILL.md (guard file -> hot) + verb-do.md (+ verb-spec.md if (b)). Behavior-preserving prose move; keep the do-loop skeleton + router + gate inline per the invariant.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) proceed — extract PR-link presentation block (SKILL.md:188-195) to verb-do.md with a one-line pointer; behavior-preserving prose move. Safe to auto-ship: blast radius is prose-only (two doc files, zero code/script paths), trivially reversible, gated by seam_check + test_live_docs_are_green; hot is by guard-file path (SKILL.md) not blast, which is what adjudicate_hot=true authorizes. Merge-time guard review stays the keystone. Place the moved section near Self-teardown in verb-do.md (section-anchored, no line cross-cites).

title: dispatcher swallows the state-rollback signal: state.read exit 1 discarded by cmd_next/cmd_finish, silent stage re-execution
description: EVIDENCE (verified 2026-06-10 @ 8fe8a86): dispatch_stage.py:306-310 and :419-424 -- ts, exit_code = state.read(td) checks only ts is None; exit_code 1 (corrupt state.json quarantined + rolled back to newest .bak, state.py:172-178, contract :184-192) is silently dropped, payload carries no marker. FAILURE SEQUENCE: state.json corrupts after finish(create_pr, completed); next advance triggers quarantine + restores the previous backup where create_pr is still in_progress; pick_next_pending (state.py:308-317: in_progress is resumable) re-dispatches create_pr; the do-loop re-executes a non-idempotent stage (second PR / duplicate comment / re-merge attempt) with no friction entry and no signal -- the rollback is observable only by diffing .bak files post-hoc. The rollback is by design; the silence is the gap. FIX: propagate a state_recovered_from_backup:true marker in the next/finish payload (mirroring the existing snapshot_write_failed marker pattern) so the do-loop can log friction and re-verify the stage before re-running it; add the verb-do.md prose handling. BLAST RADIUS: dispatch_stage.py + state.py + verb-do.md (guard files, hot).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) proceed. Right fix and safe to auto-ship (additive marker mirroring snapshot_write_failed; blast radius is one payload key, not control flow; reversible; CI-covered by new tests; hot but adjudicate_hot=true on this maintainer self-target). state.read already returns exit_code 1 on rollback, so state.py is NOT touched (bead blast-radius line was speculative). Drift verified on origin/main. MANDATORY implementer commitments folded in: (1) the state_recovered_from_backup marker MUST ride cmd_finish's ERROR return (475-476) as well as its success return, AND cmd_advance MUST merge finish_payload's marker on the success path (cmd_next's post-restore read returns exit 0, so a finish-detected rollback is otherwise lost) -- this is the exact path the bead's failure sequence hits; (2) the regression test must REPRODUCE the real rollback (valid state + valid .bak rotated, then corrupt state.json, assert advance/next/finish carry the marker and the rolled-back stage is re-picked), not inject the marker; (3) verb-do.md prose must name the non-idempotent stages (create_pr/merge/commit) that require re-verification before re-run. New friction type STATE_ROLLBACK added to flow_friction.py VALID_TYPES (no enumeration test breaks). Note (out of scope): finish_stage has no status guard, so it marks a rolled-back pending stage completed without raising -- not fixed here.

title: recover.reload_snapshot reports success when the snapshot write failed (suppressed exception, unconditional snapshot_reloaded:true)
description: EVIDENCE (verified 2026-06-10 @ 8fe8a86): recover.py:119-122 -- with contextlib.suppress(Exception): write_snapshot(...) followed by unconditional return 0, {"snapshot_reloaded": True}. FAILURE SEQUENCE: run aborts at next with config/version drift; the hint says /flow recover --reload-snapshot; write_snapshot raises (transient OSError, vanished tracked file mid-_tree_hash -- the same class classify_drift fails closed on, snapshot.py:395-397); exception swallowed, CLI prints success; old sha remains; next advance aborts with the identical drift message contradicting the tool's own report. In an --auto run the agent trusts the JSON and burns a retry loop. cmd_init was already hardened against this exact pattern (dispatch_stage.py:181-198 surfaces snapshot-write failure loudly); reload-snapshot, the designated remediation, was left silent. FIX: drop the suppress; return rc 1 + the error on write failure. BLAST RADIUS: recover.py only (the remediation path the drift-abort hint points every operator/agent at). Fail-noisy change, small.

title: recover.abort unlinks run.lock unflocked with no live-check; takeover does stage resets after releasing the flock
description: EVIDENCE (verified 2026-06-10 @ 8fe8a86): recover.py:111-116 abort does bare lock.exists() + lock.unlink(missing_ok=True) -- no flock, no classify; sibling takeover (:76-80) routes through takeover_clear and refuses live. test_recover.py:167 test_abort_removes_lock asserts removal of a LIVE lease (TTL 600, just acquired) -- the no-live-check behavior is baked in. FAILURE SEQUENCE: operator decides run A is wedged, issues /flow recover abort T; between decision and unlink A finishes and releases; drain launches run B which acquires fresh; abort's unlink deletes B's LIVE lease. B never notices: _guard_lease_ownership (dispatch_stage.py:280-281) treats lease-gone as proceed-without-one, so B continues leaseless while the ticket is open to a third acquirer -- full de-mutex with zero signal. SECONDARY: takeover does force_stage_status resets + write_snapshot (recover.py:83-90) AFTER takeover_clear releases the flock; an acquire landing in the gap gets its just-begun stage forced back to pending (clearing started_at_sha that diff_since_stage needs) and its snapshot baseline overwritten. FIX (tighten-only): route abort through classify_then/takeover_clear-style single-flock with live-refusal (explicit --force to kill live); move takeover's resets inside the flock or re-verify lease absence after them. Update the test to assert refusal-on-live. BLAST RADIUS: recover.py + lease.py seam (safety machinery, hot).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) proceed — tighten recover.abort + recover.takeover. Extend lease.takeover_clear(force, on_cleared): abort routes through it with live-refusal default + --force escape hatch (fixes primary unflocked-unlink de-mutex); takeover passes stage-resets+snapshot as on_cleared so they run inside the lease flock (fixes secondary post-flock-release reset window). Safe to auto-ship: narrow blast (recover.py + lease.py, takeover_clear has one non-test caller), reversible, hot but adjudicate_hot on so it ships via merge-time guard review + CI. Verified no-op-free against origin/main (byte-identical) and no AB-BA (state.py never takes the lease flock). Mandatory implementer commitments: add a test asserting on_cleared runs while the lease flock is held (fcntl LOCK_NB BlockingIOError); keep takeover refuse-only (no force).

title: cmd_init discards a successfully bak-recovered state: exit 1 treated as no-valid-state, restored state.json clobbered with fresh all-pending run
description: EVIDENCE (verified 2026-06-10 @ 8fe8a86): dispatch_stage.py:138-141 have_valid = existing is not None and exit_code == 0; run_id minted fresh when exit_code != 0. But state.py:172-178 _read_locked has, by the time it returns (recovered, 1), already quarantined the corrupt file AND written the recovered state back to disk; state.init (state.py:204-220, called at dispatch_stage.py:211) overwrites unconditionally. FAILURE SEQUENCE: completed run, lease released; state.json later corrupts; operator runs /flow do T -> read quarantines + restores from .bak (all stages completed, valid on disk) -> returns exit 1 -> have_valid False -> fresh run_id -> lease acquires (none present) -> state.init wipes the restored history to all-pending -> pipeline replays implement on a shipped ticket: duplicate branch work, duplicate PR. The recovery machinery worked and init threw the result away in the same call. No test covers init with a bak-recovered state (grep test_dispatch_stage.py for quarantine/bak: zero hits). FIX: treat exit_code 1 as valid-for-resume (the read healed the file), or refuse with 'state recovered from backup, run /flow recover detect'. Never fall through to fresh init. BLAST RADIUS: dispatch_stage.py + state.py contract (guard files, hot).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) proceed. Fix cmd_init to treat state.read exit 1 as valid-for-resume (have_valid = exit_code in (0,1); run_id keyed off have_valid), so a bak-recovered state is resumed not clobbered. Option A over refusing: consistent with next/finish/advance which already proceed on exit 1 (PR#292). Safe to auto-ship: maintainer self-target, adjudicate_hot=true, narrow guard-file blast radius (cmd_init only), reversible, CI-covered. Mandatory implementer commitments: (1) RED-FIRST regression test mirroring the ticket's exact failure sequence - release the lease then re-init with NO nonce/no force (the 'none present' path, not same-session re-entry) - run it red against current code before the fix; (2) surface state_recovered_from_backup marker from cmd_init AND wire verb-do.md's init exit-code section to honor it (STATE_ROLLBACK friction + re-verify the first non-idempotent stage), since an init-recovered .bak can be one write behind and would otherwise replay a landed create_pr/commit = the duplicate PR this ticket targets.

title: resume launders mid-suspension drift: cmd_init unconditionally re-baselines the canonical snapshot
description: EVIDENCE (verified 2026-06-10 @ 8fe8a86): dispatch_stage.py:177-198 write_snapshot runs on EVERY init incl. the resuming path (:200), with no prior classify_drift (the try/except only handles write failure). FAILURE SEQUENCE: run takes snapshot S0; session dies mid-pipeline; while suspended the exact threats the snapshot exists for land (git pull swaps the engine -- the flow-2pp marketplace window -- or workspace.toml handlers are rewritten); operator resumes via /flow do T -> init recomputes the snapshot from the swapped content and overwrites snapshot.sha -> next reports match. The TOCTOU guard only protects within an uninterrupted session; every crash/resume cycle silently accepts unowned drift that next would have aborted on. The owned-drift reconcile and /flow recover --reload-snapshot exist precisely so unowned drift requires explicit acceptance -- resume bypasses both. FIX: when resuming and a snapshot exists, classify_drift FIRST; abort (or at minimum emit a drift_rebaselined marker the do-loop logs as friction) instead of silently re-baselining. BLAST RADIUS: dispatch_stage.py (guard file, hot). Bg --auto runs resume routinely here.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) proceed. Fix flow-qwf3 by making cmd_init NOT re-baseline the canonical snapshot on the RESUME path (when a snapshot.sha already exists); preserve the original S0 so next's existing TOCTOU drift guard fires correctly. This is strictly better than abort-in-init: init acquires the lease before it could classify drift, so an init-side abort would strand the held lease (do-loop step 3 skips release on exit 1), whereas deferring to next breaks the do-loop to step 5 and releases unconditionally, reusing next's correct ordering (drift gate -> lease guard -> owned-reconcile). Hot (dispatch_stage.py guard file) but safe to auto-ship: blast radius 2 files under scripts/ (no seam/MODULE.md/prose impact), reversible (recover --reload-snapshot escape hatch + false negatives accepted in this repo), CI-gated. Known consequence: resume stops masking a skill_root-anchor mismatch, but bg --auto resolves dispatch via the absolute main-checkout path so init-S0 and resume-next share one skill_root (flow-mozu not reopened). Planned: dispatch_stage.py + tests/test_dispatch_stage.py (new resume-no-launder reproduction test + repurpose test_init_snapshot_write_failure_fail_closed to assert resume skips the write).

title: lease identity has no per-session component: second /flow do on the same ticket passes the mutex against a LIVE lease
description: EVIDENCE (verified 2026-06-10 @ 8fe8a86): dispatch_stage.py:137-141 cmd_init reuses run_id from state.json on resume; lease.py:241-252 acquire checks existing.run_id == run_id BEFORE expiry (owner re-acquire succeeds on a live lease); lease.py:329-337 assert_lease_still_mine checks only run_id/boot/hostname (pid informational, lease.py:62). FAILURE SEQUENCE: bg --auto run A mid-merge-stage CI wait, lease live and heartbeating (#231). Operator misjudges it dead, runs /flow do T in the same worktree: B's init reads the same state.json -> same run_id -> owner-re-acquire branch passes despite the live lease. Every later guard is blind (identical run_id/boot/host); either side's release drops the other's live lease; A and B interleave begin/finish on one state.json -> double-executed non-idempotent stages (duplicate commits/PR/merge). This is the exact 'two concurrent /flow do on one ticket' the lease.py:1 docstring claims to prevent; #231 closed the drain-reap path only. FIX DIRECTION (tighten-only): mint a per-acquire session nonce stored in the lease and carried by the dispatching session (not derivable from state.json); owner re-acquire of a LIVE lease requires the nonce; --force/takeover rotates it. BLAST RADIUS: lease.py + dispatch_stage.py (guard files, hot, every dispatch call). Needs corpus/test coverage for acquire/re-acquire/takeover paths.

title: lagging-main checkout falsely trips 'drift: engine' at first next for worktree-invoked self-target runs (flow-mozu mechanism)
description: EVIDENCE: knowledge.jsonl line 226 + friction.jsonl line 49 (flow-mozu, 2026-06-10): spec->EnterWorktree->do run tripped 'drift: engine' at the FIRST next (pre-edit) because the snapshot engine basis (worktree cut at origin/main) diverged from the lagging local main checkout that _engine_component re-hashes (snapshot.py:122-143 resolves engine_root via git worktree list -> main checkout); recovered via manual recover.py reload-snapshot. flow-s9w close comment explicitly carves this out as a separate mechanism warranting its own bead. PR#240 mitigates only the drain launch path (batch-boundary advance); interactive spec launches from a stale checkout remain exposed. CONSTRAINT (from flow-s9w adjudication): do NOT make the engine component owned-reconcilable and do NOT add per-file engine storage -- both re-open the flow-2pp TOCTOU hole. Candidate fixes: (a) verb-spec launch prose advances the main checkout (ff-only) before bootstrap when self-targeting, or (b) snapshot records the engine basis ref and init verifies basis==current-main before baselining, failing with an actionable 'advance the checkout' message instead of a generic drift abort. VALUE: removes one manual recover round-trip per stale-checkout launch. BLAST RADIUS: snapshot.py and/or dispatch_stage.py (guard files -> hot) or spec prose.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
flow --auto could not self-approve: the work item rests on a mechanism that does not reproduce, and the only behavior-changing fix is net-negative in this repo. Deferring for a maintainer re-scope. Corrected findings (verified empirically this run, local main was 24 commits behind origin/main):

1. PREMISE IS FALSE — static lag alone trips NO drift. `snapshot._engine_component` always hashes the MAIN checkout's engine tree (resolves main_root from `git worktree list` first stanza, identically for init's write_snapshot and next's classify_drift). With the main checkout NOT moving, stored == current, both hash the same lagging local main, so a worktree-invoked self-target run launched from a stale checkout does NOT drift at first `next`. The friction line-49 note ("snapshot engine basis = worktree origin/main diverged from main") was a loose in-flight scribble; both sides compute against the main checkout.

2. REAL TRIGGER — the engine guard anchors on the LOCAL MAIN CHECKOUT, a mutable moving target. `drift: engine` fires only when local main HEAD MOVES (a `git pull` / marketplace update / mid-batch advance) BETWEEN snapshot-write and a later `next`. Same class as flow-qmtd friction line 69 ("main checkout HEAD advanced 6dac335->f72bc95 mid-run"). This is a TRUE positive w.r.t. the loaded engine code, a false positive only w.r.t. the run's frozen content.

3. CORRECT FIX IS CONSTRAINED OUT — anchoring the guard on the frozen worktree engine copy is off-limits per flow-s9w (re-opens the flow-2pp TOCTOU; flow-2pp deliberately anchors on the main checkout to catch mid-run marketplace swaps, since the running session loads engine code from the main checkout).

4. CANDIDATE (a) IS NET-NEGATIVE HERE — "advance local main ff-only before bootstrap" makes every launch a mover of the SHARED local main checkout. Today `gh pr merge` advances origin but NOT the local main working tree, so during a `concurrency=8` drain batch local main stays static and in-flight siblings stay quiet. With (a), each launch ff-advances local main to a still-advancing origin, moving the snapshot basis under siblings baselined at the older commit -> per-launch `drift: engine` cascade across in-flight siblings. This is exactly the hazard the EXISTING "Pre-launch advance (clean-boundary, conditional)" gate (references/verb-evolve.md:224) already guards against: the drain advances the checkout ONLY when nothing is mid-pipeline, precisely to avoid swapping the checkout under in-flight runs. Candidate (a) advances per-bootstrap under live siblings, regressing that invariant.

5. CANDIDATE (b) — "snapshot records engine basis ref + init emits an actionable 'advance the checkout' message" — is messaging-only: it does NOT prevent the drift abort (the run still halts + needs manual recover), and it edits MORE/hotter guard files (snapshot.py + dispatch_stage.py). Strictly weaker than the status quo for the autonomous-run goal.

MAINTAINER DECISION NEEDED (re-scope): given the premise is false, the correct fix is constrained out, (a) backfires under concurrency, and (b) is messaging-only — options are (i) close wontfix and accept the rare manual `recover.py reload-snapshot` round-trip as the cost of the flow-2pp guard; (ii) re-scope to candidate (b) messaging-only if a clearer abort message is judged worth a hot PR; (iii) re-scope to a different idea entirely (e.g. detect a benign forward fast-forward of the engine vs an arbitrary swap — but that is near the owned-reconcilable line flow-s9w forbids, needs careful design). To unstick: decide here, reopen (status->open), and re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan interactively.

title: triage.py decided probe misses maintainer decisions recorded with a non-canonical stem
description:
MACHINERY (major). The --auto decided-mode probe (triage.py:119 _recorded_decision, _DECISION_STEMS = ('TRIAGE-DECISION:','DECISION:') at triage.py:40) start-anchors on startswith(stem). On flow-rvc the maintainer recorded the decision as a comment starting 'MAINTAINER DECISION 2026-06-10: ...', which matches NO stem (it does not start with DECISION:/TRIAGE-DECISION:, and a literal 'MAINTAINER DECISION:' stem would still miss it because a date sits between DECISION and the colon). So triage.py decided returned decided:false on a genuinely-decided HOT bead, and the run only shipped because the adjudication path (which honors adjudicate_hot) ran instead of the decided short-circuit.

TWO LAYERS:
(a) STEM/CONVENTION GAP: a freeform 'MAINTAINER DECISION <date>:' is undetectable by an exact-prefix stem set. Fix direction is a maintainer judgment, NOT a trivial tuple add: either (i) codify that maintainer decisions MUST use the canonical 'DECISION:'/'TRIAGE-DECISION:' stem (cheap, doc-only, but relies on discipline), or (ii) make _recorded_decision tolerant of a 'MAINTAINER DECISION ...:' prefix via a small regex (e.g. ^(MAINTAINER )?(TRIAGE-)?DECISION\b[^:]*:). Option (ii) widens what counts as a decision stem, so it needs a maintainer call + a regression test in tests/test_triage.py.
(b) DEEPER DESIGN GAP (the real teeth): even with a matching stem, the decided short-circuit (verb-spec.md step 5 'the decided short-circuit still wins first ... is_hot true -> block') does NOT honor [evolve] adjudicate_hot=true — only the adjudication path does. So a maintainer who writes a decision on a HOT bead AND sets adjudicate_hot=true (intending 'auto-drainable hot', exactly flow-rvc's stated intent) gets a BLOCK on relaunch, the opposite of intent. Here it shipped ONLY because the probe missed the decision. Consider making the decided short-circuit honor adjudicate_hot for hot decided beads (route hot+decided -> proceed when adjudicate_hot, mirroring the adjudication branch's is_hot_change hard-floor gate at verb-spec.md step 5.3).

EVIDENCE: triage.py:40 (_DECISION_STEMS), triage.py:119-138 (_recorded_decision startswith), triage.py:141-173 (decided()), verb-spec.md step 5 Decided sub-branch + advisor-adjudication branch step 3 (adjudicate_hot floor). Friction logged on the flow-rvc run (run_id 6c226010b31313e0, BLOCKER/major). corpus delta: not scored — no concrete edit yet (both fix directions are maintainer judgments).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) Ship BOTH layers together (option (ii) regex, not doc-only). Verdict proceed; non-hot (planned files triage.py/verb-spec.md/verb-triage.md/test_triage.py, none in _GUARD_FILES; probe is_hot:false). Rationale: layer (a) alone REGRESSES the adjudicate_hot=true intent (a now-detected hot decided bead would route to block), so a+b are ship-both-or-neither. Mandatory in-run implementer commitments: (1) triage.py _recorded_decision -> anchored CASE-SENSITIVE regex ^(?:MAINTAINER\s+)?(?:TRIAGE-)?DECISION\b[^:\n]*: (canonical DECISION:/TRIAGE-DECISION: still match; freeform 'MAINTAINER DECISION <date>:' now matches). (2) flow_worktree.py DELEGATES to triage.decided (verified line 451) so layer (a) propagates free, no extra file, stays non-hot. (3) Layer (b) is prose-only: gate the decided short-circuit's is_hot-true case on triage.py adjudicate-hot-enabled (true->proceed CI+merge-guard-gated, false->block unchanged), mirroring verb-spec step 5.3. Must update EVERY restatement or the spec self-contradicts: verb-spec.md Decided sub-branch (is_hot true -> block, ~origin/main:183) + its advisor-adjudication restatement (~:196), AND verb-triage.md:50-51 + the 'Lifting the hot floor' section (~:90-97). (4) Regression test in tests/test_triage.py for the freeform stem. Gate: CI + merge-time review.

title: commit-stage git apply --cached fails on untrack-only ticket: capture leaves staged deletion, index not clean before apply
description: MACHINERY (major). An untrack-only ticket (git index-untrack-cached of a tracked file + add it to .gitignore, working copy kept; e.g. flow-kwai) breaks the commit stage step-5 'git apply --cached --binary implement.diff' with 'does not exist in index'. Root: capture-implement-diff deliberately carves the staged deletion out of its finally reset (diff_extract.py:348-353 carve-out, :376-377 reset only touches untracked intent-to-add paths; the comment states it leaves the index exactly as it was found). So the deletion stays STAGED after capture, and the commit stage then applies a HEAD-relative patch against a dirty index -> git error. The deletion MUST be staged when capture runs (else git diff HEAD omits it, verified empirically), but the index MUST be clean (==HEAD) when apply runs (same patch applies clean against a clean index, verified). PR#239 fixed capture's _IgnoredPlannedFile guard for exactly this scenario but left this downstream apply broken; flow-kwai's TRIAGE-DECISION ('the commit gate now passes it') was therefore incomplete -- it shipped only because the run worked around it by hand. FIX OPTIONS: (1) PRIMARY, low blast, prose: references/stage-commit.md, insert a 'git reset' (mixed, to HEAD) between step-2 capture and step-5 apply -- no-op for normal tickets (index already HEAD), and it matches stage-implement.md's own pre-flight-the-commit-gate note ('git reset first if you staged anything'). (2) ALTERNATIVE, hot script: diff_extract.py capture finally also resets the carved-out staged_deleted paths so index==HEAD after the diff is written; needs staged_deleted lifted out of the 'if untracked' scope. Recommend (1): it does not change capture's documented observe-and-leave-as-found contract and avoids touching a hot guard file. ADD A REGRESSION TEST either way (an untrack-only fixture: tracked file + gitignore -> capture -> apply against clean index succeeds and stages both the deletion and the gitignore edit). In-run workaround proven this run: reset between capture and apply -> commit f3f704d, PR #242 green. corpus delta: not scored -- primary fix is references prose (cannot move the four pure deciders); the diff_extract.py variant is a candidate, not yet a concrete tested edit. friction id b4becf9c; knowledge entry f7abf3424a18b13d.
