VALUE: removes a footgun -- if a workspace wires create_pr to a real handler and trusts the registry's required_fields the way commit/implement do, create_pr blocks forever (pr_title never populated). RESOLUTION (established contract = remove the field): align the registry to the code+doc by dropping required_fields from create_pr; the no-gate behavior is the independently-established contract (stage-create_pr.md + create_pr.py both assert it). BLAST: latent -- create_pr defaults to handler 'none', so it only springs in a GitHub/skill-wired workspace whose author follows the registry over the doc. HOT: edits stage-registry.toml.

title: lease.py crashes every caller on a corrupt run.lock; no quarantine analog to state.py
description: ROBUSTNESS gap (tighten-only, asymmetry). read_lease raises LeaseError on an unparseable/corrupt run.lock (lease.py:178-179), caught ONLY inside lease.py's own CLI (lease.py:454,475,484,493 -> exit 3). Every LIBRARY caller is unguarded against the bare LeaseError: dispatch_stage.py:168,174,271,341,376 catch only the typed subclasses LeaseHeld/LeaseExpiredForeign/LeaseLost; status.py:58, recover.py:62/88, flow_worktree.py:270 catch nothing. So cmd_init (dispatch_stage.py:157), 'recover detect' (recover.py:62), 'recover takeover' (recover.py:88), /flow status, and worktree cleanup all crash with a raw traceback on a corrupt lease -- the diagnostic for this exact failure crashes on it. Only 'recover abort' (recover.py:118-123) survives, because it blind-unlinks run.lock. ASYMMETRY: state.py:150-180 deliberately treats a corrupt state.json as recoverable (quarantine -> .bak fallback -> clean exit 1/2); lease.py has NO quarantine path. Identical corruption class, one self-heals, the other takes down its callers. Realistic trigger: schema evolution (_deserialize lease.py:151-155 reads run_id/acquired_at/lease_expires_at as required keys -> any future required-key change KeyErrors on every pre-existing on-disk lease) or external/disk corruption. VALUE: a corrupt lease becomes a recoverable, diagnosable state (via 'recover detect') instead of a crash. BLAST: per-ticket; wedges init/status/recover detect/recover takeover/worktree-cleanup until a human runs the blind 'recover abort'. TIGHTEN-ONLY: add a quarantine path mirroring state.py; do NOT weaken any lease guarantee. HOT: lease.py is named safety machinery -> rides the section-6A property-check review.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
flow --auto could not self-approve (HOT lease/mutex machinery). Planner produced a strong tighten-only plan but rated 86% and raised a clarifying question whose answer changes the code:

OPEN QUESTION
- On 'recover takeover' against a corrupt run.lock, blind-unlink it (consistent with 'recover abort' today) OR rename to run.lock.quarantine.<ts> for post-mortem forensics? The rename is the closer state.py-style quarantine analog the ticket asks for and is mutex-safe here (takeover already refused if the lease were live), but adds a file the recover flow must reason about. Pick one before code is written.

RESIDUAL DESIGN ITEMS to confirm interactively
- evolve_drain.py corrupt-lease handling was asserted from grep, not read end-to-end; it gates a self-merge, so the 'corrupt' state MUST be made blocking (like live), never default into the parked->done->merge branch. Verify the exact predicate + human-surfacing.
- dispatch_stage.py refusal exit codes for a bare LeaseError must match the module's existing convention (init 1/5, lease-lost 7, lease CLI 3 for bare LeaseError).

CORE DESIGN (sound, for reference): read_lease STAYS raising on corrupt (no silent steal on the acquire/refresh mutation path); add a 'corrupt' state to lease.classify (try/except LeaseError -> {state: corrupt, holder: None}), which transitively fixes status/recover detect/worktree-cleanup; give every '== live' consumer an explicit 'corrupt' branch; dispatch callers catch the bare LeaseError and refuse cleanly with a /flow recover hint.

To unstick: answer the open question here, reopen (status->open), and re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan interactively.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: RENAME, not blind-unlink. On recover-takeover against a corrupt run.lock, rename it to run.lock.quarantine.TIMESTAMP for post-mortem forensics (mutex-safe; takeover already refuses if the lease is live). Also make evolve_drain.py corrupt-lease handling BLOCKING (treat corrupt like live, never default-permissive) since it gates a self-merge; read it end-to-end rather than assume from grep.

title: no CLI test for dispatch_stage advance with malformed --skill-output
description:
GROUND TRUTH (missing test on a hot-path error branch). dispatch_stage cli_main 'advance' parses --skill-output as JSON (dispatch_stage.py:552-553) and errors on malformed JSON (553-554); the skill-output validation is at 516-526. advance is the primary do-loop call (every /flow run, SKILL.md:167). tests/test_dispatch_stage.py exercises cmd_advance directly but has no test calling cli_main(['advance', ...]) with --skill-output, so the malformed-JSON error path and its exit-code routing through cli_main are unverified. A regression there could mis-handle skill-stage output silently on the hot path.

FIX: add a CLI-level test invoking cli_main(['advance', ..., '--skill-output', '<not json>']) asserting the error path + exit code; add a happy-path --skill-output round-trip. Test-only; anchored on the test file so it lands as a bare leaf.
BLAST RADIUS: tests/test_dispatch_stage.py. New test only; no production code touched.

title: no test for flow_friction LockContention exit code 2
description:
GROUND TRUTH (missing test on a documented contract). flow_friction.py cli_main can raise LockContention -> exit code 2 (flow_friction.py:139-141; the flock_retry call at line 98 is the trigger). SKILL prose / verb-do.md document exit 2 = lock contention as part of the friction-append CLI contract. tests/test_flow_friction.py (8 tests) covers exit 4 (test_cli_missing_config_returns_4) but NEVER triggers LockContention, so the exit-2 contract is unverified. Lock contention during friction append is a real failure mode in a parallel-run fleet.

FIX: add a test that holds the friction flock from another process (the multiprocessing pattern test_lease.py already uses) and asserts cli_main returns 2. Test-only change; anchored on the test file (NOT flow_friction.py) so it lands as a bare leaf, not a guard-file hot change.
BLAST RADIUS: tests/test_flow_friction.py. New test only; no production code touched.

title: inventory.md API/contract tables drifted from code (exit codes, comment schema, missing subcommand)
description:
GROUND TRUTH (doc drifted from code; code is the established contract). Four stale entries in scripts/inventory.md, all verified against current code:

1. tracker_cli exit codes incomplete. inventory.md:735 says '0=ok, 1=tracker error, 2=workspace config invalid, 3=invalid args' (4 codes). tracker_cli.py:24-30 documents 0-5: code 4 (hard transition failure: permission_denied/validator_failed/missing_required_field) and code 5 (transition not applicable: wrong_source_state/ambiguous_transition) are missing. stage-commit.md:100 relies on exit 5 being a valid non-fatal outcome.

2. comment-wrap schema wrong. inventory.md:731 says comment wraps body as '{format: markdown, value: text}'. tracker_cli.py:180-184 wraps as {"body": args.text, "fmt": "md"}; tracker.py Content TypedDict is {body: str, fmt: Literal['md','adf','plain']}. Different field names + a fmt enum, not a freeform format string.

3. dispatcher exit codes. inventory.md:419-422 shows exit 0,1,2,7 with exit 7 marked 'RESERVED (phase 7-full)' and no row for exit 5. dispatch_stage.py:15-21 documents 0,1,2,5,7; both 5 (LeaseExpiredForeign, lease.py:43 / dispatch_stage.py:175) and 7 (EXIT_LEASE_LOST, dispatch_stage.py:377) are live code paths. SKILL.md:98 contracts exit 5. The 'RESERVED' label on 7 is stale.

4. missing subcommand. inventory.md:725-733 lists 7 tracker_cli subcommands but omits download-attachments (tracker_cli.py:292-300), which stage-ticket.md:44-48 depends on and MODULE.md already lists.

FIX: update the four inventory.md tables to match code. Behavior-preserving (doc only). Consolidated into one bead because all four share basename inventory.md (separate beads would fuzzy-collide on the same-file dedup pass).
BLAST RADIUS: scripts/inventory.md. Doc-only, no code change, high reviewability.

title: snapshot drift guard fails open when sha write is interrupted or swallowed
description:
GROUND TRUTH: the canonical-snapshot TOCTOU guard can silently disable itself for the rest of a run.

write_snapshot (scripts/snapshot.py:182-196) writes snapshot.json FIRST, then snapshot.sha. The emit call site dispatch_stage.py:181-184 wraps the whole call in contextlib.suppress(Exception) (comment: 'a snapshot write failure must not block the run; verify treats absence as no-op'). So if the SECOND write (sha) raises (disk-full / EINTR / interrupt) after json already landed, the exception is swallowed and the on-disk state is json-present / sha-absent. It is ALSO reachable on a hard crash (SIGKILL/power-loss) in the window between the two atomic_write_text calls.

classify_drift (scripts/snapshot.py:244-245): 'if not sha_path.exists(): return True, "no snapshot to verify", []' -> ok=True. dispatch_stage._gate_drift then reads drift_ok=True and every later 'dispatch next' proceeds with the drift guard OFF for the rest of the run: workspace.toml swaps, handler-code swaps, registry edits all go undetected. The 'absence is a safe no-op' assumption only holds when BOTH files are absent (run never emitted); it is violated by the reachable json-present/sha-absent partial state.

FIX (tightens, never erodes): in write_snapshot, write snapshot.sha BEFORE snapshot.json. A partial-write survivor is then sha-present/json-absent, and classify_drift recomputes current vs stored sha -> fail-CLOSED (match keeps the guard active; a real drift returns mismatch). Optional hardening: in classify_drift, treat json-present/sha-absent as drift rather than 'no snapshot'. ~2 lines, no signature change; both write_snapshot call sites (dispatch_stage.py:184,282) benefit. HOT: snapshot.py is a named safety-machinery guard file -> must ride the hot path and clear the section-6A property-check review (the order swap strengthens fail-open->fail-closed, so it passes).

EVIDENCE: scripts/snapshot.py:182-196 (write order), scripts/snapshot.py:244-245 (sha-absent fail-open), dispatch_stage.py:181-184 (suppress wrapping both writes).
BLAST RADIUS: scripts/snapshot.py. ~2-line reorder in one function; no API change; restores a guard property. Tighten-only.

title: evolve --auto park should file-a-bead-and-exit instead of stalling the fleet
description: Follow-up to flow-bly (which documented the interim PTY-attach recipe in verb-evolve 6C, PR #63). The genuinely-unattended fix: when an --auto evolve run cannot self-approve at >=90% (degrades to interactive / parks on AskUserQuestion or ExitPlanMode), instead file a bead capturing the open question(s) and EXIT cleanly, so a --ship drain never stalls waiting on a human/PTY. The parked decision becomes new backlog work the loop re-picks once answered. Touches --auto semantics in references/verb-spec.md (the sub-90% / clarifying-questions branch) and the do-loop's AskUserQuestion handling — HOT, larger blast radius than the doc-only flow-bly. Evidence: flow-bly description (flow-aut.5 parked twice; only a hand-rolled PTY attach could answer); advisor on flow-bly explicitly flagged option 3 as the real unattended fix.
close_reason: Superseded by flow-bly (PR #64, merged). bly shipped the genuine fix: --auto runs DEFER-AND-EXIT (defer ticket in place + comment + exit, drops from bd ready). 2cr proposed the file-a-NEW-bead variant; defer-in-place is strictly better (no loop re-entry, no bead proliferation). Goal (drain never stalls on a park) achieved.

title: drift guard aborts maintainer tickets that edit their own .flow/workspace.toml
description: MACHINERY (major): a maintainer self-target ticket that legitimately edits .flow/workspace.toml (e.g. adding an [evolve] key) trips the canonical-snapshot drift guard on the post-implement dispatch_stage advance/next ('drift: workspace_toml', exit 1), forcing a manual /flow recover reload-snapshot mid-run before the loop can continue. The TOCTOU guard is load-bearing and SHOULD flag unexpected config changes, but an edit to a file that is in the run's own planned_files is owned/intended, not external tampering. Fix candidate: the do-loop (or dispatch_stage) auto-reloads the snapshot when the only drifted path is a planned_file for this ticket, instead of aborting to recover. Repro: flow-kos (advance after implement). BLAST RADIUS: scripts/dispatch_stage.py + SKILL.md do-loop. Structural dispatch change, high cross-fleet blast radius — propose, do not self-apply.
close_reason: Merged via PR #66 (squash). dispatch_stage now auto-reconciles an OWNED single-component workspace.toml drift (sole drifted component is workspace_toml AND .flow/workspace.toml in planned_files, lease-confirmed) -> reload snapshot baseline + continue with reconciled_drift marker, instead of aborting maintainer tickets that edit their own config. Genuine/multi-component drift still aborts; TOCTOU guard preserved. seam_check green.

title: capture-implement-diff leaks [memory].root abs path when workspace.toml is in planned_files
description: MACHINERY (major): scripts/diff_extract.py capture_implement_diff diffs HEAD-blob vs working tree over planned_files. flow_worktree._set_memory_root (scripts/flow_worktree.py:103) injects [memory].root=<main .flow abs path> into the worktree workspace.toml at bootstrap. When a (maintainer) ticket lists .flow/workspace.toml in planned_files, that machine-local absolute root line rides into implement.diff and would commit to origin/main. resolve_memory_base (scripts/_memory_paths.py:38) only reads [memory].root from workspace.toml, so the redirect cannot move out-of-band. Repro: flow-kos. Fix candidates: (a) capture-implement-diff strips the [memory].root line from any workspace.toml hunk; (b) move the redirect to a gitignored sibling that _memory_paths also consults. Workaround used in flow-kos: strip-capture-commit-restore. BLAST RADIUS: scripts/diff_extract.py (or scripts/_memory_paths.py + flow_worktree.py). HOT FILE: capture-implement-diff is on every run's commit path.
close_reason: Merged via PR #65 (squash). diff_extract no longer leaks the machine-local [memory].root abs path into implement.diff when workspace.toml is in planned_files.

title: Wire the new verb into the SKILL.md router (HOT)
description: Wire 'new' into SKILL.md: add it to the exact-token verb set + router table (-> references/verb-new.md), alongside init/spec/do/recall/status. Keep SKILL.md thin (one table row + one sentence; detail in the ref). Confirm 'new' doesn't collide with the bare-ticket-key->spec fallback (exact-token match, same guarantee as sync vs sync-42). Acceptance: /flow new routes to verb-new.md, bare key still -> spec, seam_check.py green. HOT: SKILL.md is the per-iteration router. Bump plugin.json + marketplace.json in sync. Depends on flow-uo7.
close_reason: Acceptance already satisfied on origin/main: dependency PR #57 (flow-uo7.1) over-delivered — added the `new` row to the SKILL.md router table (line 31, routes to references/verb-new.md), bare ticket keys still default to spec (untouched, exact-token match keeps `new` distinct from a `new-…` key), and seam_check.py is green (0 errors/0 warnings). Plugin/marketplace versions also already bumped (now 0.25.11). Narrative-prose omission of `new` is not a gap: init and evolve are likewise table-only. No PR — closing as already-done per user decision (/flow --auto run).

title: Add verb-new.md authoring reference for the /flow new front door
description: Add references/verb-new.md: a /flow new authoring front door. Resolves workspace via tracker_cli.py --workspace-root . (same universal pattern as verb-status/spec), collects PROBLEM-only inputs (summary + short context + type) via AskUserQuestion, subprocesses 'tracker_cli.py --workspace-root . create ...' (subcommand from flow-7ar), surfaces the new key, ends by offering /flow <newkey> to spec it. NO solution/plan (spec stage's job). Acceptance: doc invokes only real tracker_cli create flags; seam_check.py + test_seam_check.py::test_live_docs_are_green green. Depends on flow-7ar landing the create subcommand.

title: version-bump target derived from launcher checkout can lag origin/main; implement must reconcile
description: MACHINERY (major): the --auto plan/spec stage stamps version-bump targets from the pre-bootstrap LAUNCHER checkout, which lags origin/main. flow-2yr plan said 0.25.6->0.25.7 but the worktree base (origin/main, @default) already had 0.25.7 (prior merged PR), so implement reconciled to 0.25.8 to keep a real delta. references/stage-implement.md (and verb-spec.md step 6) should instruct computing the bump against the WORKTREE BASE plugin.json (origin/main current version), not the plan's stated number. Evidence: friction RECONCILE entry b9d303ae on run 54e0b314; knowledge DEVIATION 313f006ab256e202.

title: commit stage: skeleton body left unfilled because Write tool blocks redirect-created file
description: references/stage-commit.md step 3 writes the commit skeleton via shell redirect (compose_commit.py > /tmp/flow-commit-KEY.txt); step 4 says 'Write the completed message back to the same path'. The Write tool refuses a file never Read in-session ('File has not been read yet'), so the body fill fails and 'git commit -F' lands the literal '# body - fill in below this line' skeleton, needing a 'git commit --amend'. Fix: stage-commit.md:64-67 should instruct Read-ing the skeleton before Write, or composing the full message and Writing fresh after a Read. Severity minor (self-corrected via amend; did not fail the run). Surfaced by flow-aut.14 run.

title: reap 'gh pr merge --delete-branch' leaks local branch + worktree (still checked out)
description: EVIDENCE (this drain): EVERY one of 12 merges printed 'failed to delete local branch feature/flow-... : cannot delete branch used by worktree at .../flow.worktrees/feature-flow-...'. Remote merge + remote branch delete succeed, but the local branch and its git worktree under flow.worktrees/ leak because the spawned run's worktree is still registered (the bg session opened it and may still be in reflect). After a full drain that is ~12 stale worktrees + branches accumulating. FIX DIRECTION: reap step should 'git worktree remove --force' + 'git branch -D' for the merged key after the session ends (or the run should self-remove its worktree on completion). BLAST: references/verb-evolve.md, scripts/flow_worktree.py (teardown). HOT: False.

title: dedup evid fingerprint too wording-sensitive: same defect refiled under a new key (9jk vs mst)
description: EVIDENCE (this drain): flow-9jk was filed describing the EXACT defect flow-mst already fixed (both: verb-spec.md:96 --auto Plan subagent reads pre-bootstrap .flow files). They got DIFFERENT evid: fingerprints (9jk evid:ea19a0a73d90 vs mst's) so the --dedup-key seam did NOT collide them; 9jk was refiled as already-fixed work (I closed it as dup). The seam is supposed to anchor on primary-file+symptom so re-discovery converges, but the symptom wording varied enough to hash differently. So Producer A (reflect sling) re-files closed/fixed issues across runs -> loop does not fully converge. FIX DIRECTION: normalize the dedup-key harder (anchor on file path + a canonical symptom token, drop free wording from the hash), or add a find-duplicates pass before filing. BLAST: plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/flow_beads_create.py. HOT: False.

title: evolve --ship parked --auto runs cannot be answered without a hand-rolled PTY (no headless path)
description:
EVIDENCE (this drain): flow-aut.5's --auto run PARKED twice (AskUserQuestion: scope, then ExitPlanMode gate) since it degraded to interactive at <90% confidence. To answer from the orchestrator: 'claude --resume <id> --print' REFUSES ('Session ... is currently running as a background agent; use claude agents to attach, or --fork-session'); --fork-session would duplicate. Only a hand-built PTY attach worked, and only after: short session id (not UUID), TIOCSWINSZ ioctl to set nonzero winsize (else 'malformed request: Too small: expected number to be >=1'), send Enter to pick the default option, Ctrl+Z to detach. verb-evolve sec 6C just says 'answer any parked sessions' with no non-TTY recipe. So unattended --ship stalls on the first park unless a human (or this PTY hack) intervenes. FIX DIRECTION: document the PTY-attach recipe in verb-evolve, OR provide a headless answer command, OR make --auto evolve runs file-a-bead-and-exit instead of parking. BLAST: references/verb-evolve.md, references/background-pipeline.md. HOT: False.

=== PINNED MAINTAINER DECISION (implement EXACTLY this; do NOT re-derive or re-fork) ===
Fix direction #3 DEFER-AND-EXIT. --auto must NEVER park. When an --auto run cannot self-approve (clarifying question / sub-90% / BAIL): defer THE ORIGINAL bead in place (status->deferred) + comment the open questions on it, then exit cleanly (bd ready excludes deferred, so the drainer stops relaunching). Do NOT mint a new follow-up bead. Scope: ALL --auto runs uniformly. Interactive (no --auto) unchanged. Files: references/verb-spec.md (--auto branch + BAIL), SKILL.md (spec gate), references/verb-evolve.md (6B/6C), references/background-pipeline.md (Blockers). Do NOT document the PTY-attach hack (fix #1, REJECTED). HOT (touches SKILL.md).
close_reason: Merged via PR #64 (squash). --auto runs now DEFER-AND-EXIT instead of parking: on a clarifying question/sub-90%/BAIL the run comments the questions on the ticket, sets it deferred (drops from bd ready, drainer stops relaunching), and exits. No more headless-park-forever. Took #3 (not the rejected PTY-doc #1). seam_check green.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION (maintainer, PINNED — implement exactly this, do NOT re-derive or re-fork): Fix direction #3 DEFER-AND-EXIT. --auto must NEVER park. When an --auto run cannot self-approve (clarifying question / sub-90% / BAIL): defer THE ORIGINAL bead in place (bd update --status deferred) + comment the open questions/residual on it, then exit cleanly (bd ready excludes deferred so the drainer stops relaunching). Do NOT mint a new follow-up bead (re-enters the loop). Scope: ALL --auto runs uniformly (the drainer launches plain /flow <key> --auto; no marker distinguishes evolve from attended). Interactive (no --auto) unchanged. Files: references/verb-spec.md (step-5 --auto branch + BAIL), SKILL.md (spec gate), references/verb-evolve.md (6B/6C), references/background-pipeline.md (Blockers). Do NOT document the PTY-attach hack (fix #1, REJECTED as papering-over). This is HOT (SKILL.md).

title: parallel evolve runs all bump plugin.json + marketplace.json version -> N-1 PRs go DIRTY
description: EVIDENCE (this drain): PR #42 (flow-aut.14) went mergeable=CONFLICTING / DIRTY purely because sibling evolve PRs merged first; the conflict was on plugins/flow/.claude-plugin/plugin.json + .claude-plugin/marketplace.json version lines (+ MODULE.md). CLAUDE.md invariant requires bumping both version files in sync on every plugin change, so EVERY concurrent evolve PR edits the same two lines -> only the first to merge stays clean, the rest conflict. Forced a close+relaunch of aut.14. STRUCTURAL: recurs on every multi-PR drain (cap 10 / concurrency 8 makes it the norm). FIX DIRECTION: either (a) evolve PRs skip the version bump and a single post-drain commit bumps once, or (b) reap auto-rebases/regenerates the version on merge, or (c) treat version files as union-merge. BLAST: references/verb-evolve.md, stage-commit.md, plugin.json/marketplace.json bump policy. HOT: True (touches the version-bump invariant in CLAUDE.md).
close_reason: Merged via PR #61 (squash). stage-implement.md now mandates deterministic patch-only version bump -> concurrent evolve PRs emit byte-identical version hunks, git 3-way auto-resolves, no more within-batch DIRTY. Cross-batch stragglers left to status-quo human path. seam_check green on main.

title: drainer reap merges the PR but never closes the bead -> merged beads stuck in_progress
description: EVIDENCE (this drain): 12 beads (flow-aut.5/8/9/12/13/15/16/17/19/20, mst, dvv) sat in status=in_progress AFTER their PRs (#35-47) squash-merged to main. I closed all 12 by hand. flow-fab (closed) added the ticket-stage open->in_progress CLAIM, but NO step does in_progress->closed. The run's reflect stage runs BEFORE evolve_reap merges the PR (reap is a separate later pass), so reflect cannot close-on-merge; and evolve_reap.py merges via 'gh pr merge' then returns, transitioning nothing. RESULT: every --ship-merged bead leaks as in_progress; backlog never shows truly drained; a human must mass-close. FIX DIRECTION: after a successful merge in the reap step (verb-evolve sec 6A / evolve_reap.py), close the bead for that key with a 'merged via PR #N' reason. BLAST: plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/evolve_reap.py, references/verb-evolve.md. HOT: False.
close_reason: Merged to main via PR #49 (squash). Manual close.

title: evolve --ship: opt-in auto-merge of HOT leaf PRs in isolation (maintainer-gated)
description:
MAINTAINER-AUTHORIZED POLICY (2026-06-03): let the drainer auto-merge hot beads too, replacing the human-merge keystone with: trusted provenance (agent/audit-authored) + well-scoped + ISOLATION (serialize hot merges, quiesce the fleet around them) + CI green + agent diff review. User: 'self-developed by you; if YOU authored the bead I trust it; several layers of reasoning throughout. Concerned about ill-scoped, not malicious.'

PLACES TO CHANGE (all harness = hot):
1. scripts/evolve_reap.py — currently classifies hot PRs as skipped_hot, never into merge[]. Add: when [evolve] auto_merge_hot is on AND the hot PR is a green LEAF AND it is isolated (no other evolve run active / it is the only mergeable hot PR this pass), include it in merge[]. Keep serialization: at most ONE hot PR per reap pass. Non-maintainer / flag-off keeps today's behavior (skipped_hot).
2. references/verb-evolve.md sec 6A — prose says 'Hot / non-green / conflicted PRs stay as draft PRs for the human.' Rewrite to: non-green/conflicted still wait; hot auto-merges only under auto_merge_hot + isolation, else waits.
3. .flow/workspace.toml [evolve] — new key auto_merge_hot (default FALSE; TRUE only in this maintainer self-target repo so the gate stays intact for user projects).
4. CLAUDE.md — note the maintainer-authorized hot-auto-merge exception beside the self-evolution/robustness keystone framing.

ISOLATION semantics: principle is serialize-at-merge (one hot at a time, quiesce around it), NOT strictly 'dead last'. A running drain pins its harness at launch so mid-flight siblings are unaffected; the real reasons are attributability/clean-revert and avoiding hot-vs-hot conflicts, plus the reflect stage reading live machinery at end-of-run (quiesce covers that).

BLAST RADIUS: scripts/evolve_reap.py, references/verb-evolve.md, .flow/workspace.toml, CLAUDE.md
HOT FILE: True (changes the safety gate; merge under the very policy it encodes, in isolation).
close_reason: Merged via PR #62 (squash). evolve_reap.py now auto-merges a hot leaf PR under [evolve] auto_merge_hot (default OFF; ON only in this maintainer repo) + code-enforced one-hot-per-pass isolation. CLAUDE.md invariant added. Gate intact for user projects. seam_check green.

title: spec --auto Plan subagent reads pre-bootstrap .flow run files that do not exist yet
description: references/verb-spec.md:96 tells the --auto Plan subagent to Read .flow/runs/<KEY>/ticket.json + .flow/tickets/<KEY>.md, but the bootstrap that writes them is shared step 6, AFTER the subagent runs in step 4. The headless Plan subagent is therefore context-starved. Working substitute: plan INLINE off tracker_cli get + direct codebase exploration (what flow-aut.14 did), reaching >=90% confidence without the broken subagent. Confirms prior finding flow-mst.
close_reason: Duplicate of flow-mst (merged PR #38). verb-spec.md:89 already inlines ticket JSON and tells the --auto Plan subagent not to read pre-bootstrap .flow files. Refiled by a run off a pre-mst base; dedup missed it on wording. Fixed on main a1f823a.

title: spec planned_files omits the TDD test file implement adds, tripping post-implement reconcile every run
description: references/verb-spec.md:56 + step 6 derive --planned-files from the plan's 'files to change' only. A TDD implement nearly always adds a NEW test file the plan didn't enumerate, so the records_diff_baseline post-implement reconcile fires on essentially every run (here: tests/test_runner.py). Fix: have the spec planner include anticipated NEW test paths (from the plan's test strategy) in --planned-files so reconcile is the exception, not the rule. Severity minor; the reconcile handled it cleanly.
close_reason: Merged to main via PR #47 (squash). Manual close (reflect backend-transition deviation).

title: sync._invoke silently drops op=create to failed; create is documented replayable so enqueued create mutations never drain
description: EVIDENCE: sync.py:11 docstring lists comment/link/create/edit as replayable best-effort. But _invoke (sync.py:65-81) has no 'create' branch, so op='create' hits the final 'return False' and reconcile() classifies it 'failed' (never dropped from pending-mutations.jsonl). create IS in pending_mutations.VALID_OPS (line 47), so it is queueable. _Tracker Protocol (sync.py:32-37) also lacks a create method. NET: an enqueued create mutation can never drain via /flow sync. FIX: add a 'create' branch to _invoke + a create method to the _Tracker Protocol (and real trackers), OR remove create from VALID_OPS/docstring if create is never enqueued. Surfaced while implementing flow-aut.12 (advisor caught it); kept out of that ticket's test-only blast radius.

title: spec --auto Plan subagent told to read pre-bootstrap ticket.json/tickets.md that do not exist yet
description: MACHINERY (major, --auto path defeated): references/verb-spec.md:96 — the --auto Plan-subagent prompt instructs 'Read .flow/runs/<KEY>/ticket.json and .flow/tickets/<KEY>.md for ticket context'. Neither exists at step 4: ticket.json is written by the ticket STAGE in the do-loop (post-bootstrap; verified this run, stage-ticket.md step 2 does 'tracker_cli get > ticket.json'), and .flow/tickets/<KEY>.md by flow_worktree.py create (bootstrap, shared step 6). Both run AFTER the --auto Plan subagent (step 4). So the subagent hits stage-plan.md's error path (ticket.json missing -> 'context unavailable, ticket stage must run first'), returns no clean NONE, and the auto-path branch (step 5) always degrades to interactive: --auto can never auto-approve. Fix needs two coordinated files: (1) verb-spec.md:90-113 should EMBED the ticket context the main agent already fetched in step 3 (tracker_cli get) into the Plan prompt instead of pointing at non-existent files; (2) the embedded stage-plan.md 'Read .flow/runs/<KEY>/ticket.json' instruction must not re-introduce the bad read pre-bootstrap. Worked around on flow-aut.18 by skipping the Plan subagent and drafting the one-line plan inline.
close_reason: Merged to main via PR #38 (squash). Beads backend not auto-transitioned by reflect (flow-7qm/fab deviation); closing manually to match shipped state.

title: flow runs branch off a stale/inconsistent base, not the fresh default-branch tip -> PRs polluted with already-merged commits (DIRTY)
description:
EVIDENCE:
First --ship dogfood: 3 runs launched after #19 merged (origin/main=cf64d6f). Their branches got DIFFERENT bases: feature/flow-aut.7 merge-base with origin/main = 8fb34f9 (#18, PRE-#19 main); feature/flow-aut.6 merge-base = cf64d6f (#19). Result: #22 and #23 carry the drainer commit (evolve_reap.py, plugin.json at old version, verb-evolve.md, MODULE.md) on top of a stale base, so vs current main they are DIRTY (merge conflict, esp. the plugin.json version line). evolve_reap correctly classified them as blocked (safety held), but they are not mergeable.

ROOT CAUSE:
flow_worktree.py does 'git worktree add -b <branch> <worktree> <base>' with --base supplied by the caller (verb-spec/do bootstrap). The caller passed a stale local ref (the launch session's local 'main' or its current HEAD) rather than the freshly-fetched origin/<default-branch> tip. So a run launched while local main lags origin (or from a feature-branch worktree) cuts its branch off the wrong commit and inherits unrelated/already-merged history.

IMPACT:
This is the drainer's blast surface: --ship launches background runs from whatever the cockpit session's checkout is. If that base is not current origin/main, every produced PR is polluted -> DIRTY -> unmergeable -> the auto-merge loop stalls on conflicts the human must untangle. Couples cleanliness of every evolve PR to the launching session's git state.

FIX DIRECTION:
Resolve --base inside the bootstrap as the freshly-fetched default-branch tip: 'git fetch origin <default>' then base = origin/<default> (read default via 'gh repo view --json defaultBranchRef' or 'git symbolic-ref refs/remotes/origin/HEAD'). Never branch off local HEAD/main for an autonomous run. Cross-check: the verb-spec/verb-do bootstrap step that computes base, and flow_worktree --base.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/flow_worktree.py, plugins/flow/skills/flow/references/verb-spec.md, plugins/flow/skills/flow/references/verb-do.md
HOT FILE: True
AUDIT SOURCES: live dogfood (first --ship)
AUDIT ID: flow-worktree-stale-base

title: drainer loop blocked: create_pr=none + no GitHub PR handler -> /flow --auto commits but never opens a PR -> reaper has nothing to merge
description:
EVIDENCE:
First /flow evolve --ship dogfood (3 launched runs) proved the auto-merge loop is broken at the PR seam. flow-aut.7 transcript (session 5d1377f2): all 9 stages completed clean, committed 2455337 (18 Jira tests), pushed feature/flow-aut.7-jira-write-method-tests — but logged 'create_pr handler none -> skip' and 'create_pr/review_loop are none in this workspace, so flow produced a commit rather than a PR'. All 3 runs pushed branches; ZERO PRs opened. gh pr list shows only #20.

ROOT CAUSE:
.flow/workspace.toml [pipeline.handlers] has create_pr='none' and review_loop='none'. stage-registry.toml create_pr: 'Bare default skips PR creation... install a PR-capable skill (ship-it, gh-pr-create) and wire it via init wizard. No inline fallback.' dispatch_stage._parse_handler accepts inline|none|subagent:X|skill:NAME. No GitHub PR skill is installed: ship-it is Bitbucket/bkt/CodeRabbit (flow repo is GitHub vdsmon/flow), and gh-pr-create is not present. So there is no wired path from a flow run to a draft PR on this repo.

IMPACT:
evolve_reap.py keys entirely on open PRs (gh pr list). With create_pr=none the drainer can launch runs but the auto-merge half is dead: nothing ever becomes a PR to reap. The earlier reap smoke returning empty was misread as 'no evolve PRs yet' — the architecture never produces them here. This is the unproven end-to-end the advisor flagged; dogfood confirms it is blocked, not merely untested.

FIX DIRECTION (decide):
(a) Write a minimal GitHub create_pr handler skill (gh pr create --draft --title <pr_title> ...) wired as create_pr='skill:<name>'; also wire review_loop so a CI/CodeRabbit wait runs and the reaper green-gate has checks to read. OR (b) have the drainer itself open the draft PR after the launched run pushes (decouples PR creation from the run's create_pr stage, but the drainer is detached from the run lifecycle). (a) is cleaner and matches the handler design. Also: references/stage-create_pr.md is referenced by the registry but MISSING (doc-drift, fold in).

NOTE: the 3 pushed branches carry real reviewable work (flow-aut.7 = 18 tests); a human can 'gh pr create' them manually meanwhile.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/.flow/workspace.toml, plugins/flow/skills/flow/references/stage-create_pr.md, plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/evolve_reap.py
HOT FILE: True
AUDIT SOURCES: live dogfood (first --ship run)
AUDIT ID: create-pr-none-blocks-drainer

title: reflect machinery lens commits self-edits directly to main (bypasses human-merge keystone)
description:
EVIDENCE:
Commit 2af2c76 ('fix(flow_worktree): exclude .claude/worktrees from config copy', author footer: 'fixed from inside the run per the reflect machinery lens', FT-1074 run) landed as a DIRECT commit on the main branch (local main checkout, unpushed). It also bumped plugin.json 0.24.0->0.24.1 off a stale base while origin/main was already 0.25.0. The same one-line fix was independently re-filed as PR #20 off current main.

PROBLEM:
The self-evolution design holds human-merge of a PR as the keystone gate (CLAUDE.md 'Robustness', plan but-wait-the-curious-puppy). A background reflect machinery self-edit that git-commits to main bypasses that gate entirely: no PR, no review, no CI, and it forks version/history off whatever stale base the run started from. Saved here only by being unpushed. This is exactly the inline-commit path the new drainer (/flow evolve --ship: friction -> evolve bead -> draft PR -> human merge) is meant to replace.

FIX DIRECTION:
Constrain the reflect machinery lens so a machinery self-edit NEVER commits to main. Either (a) apply in-place via machinery_edit.py and leave it as a working change for the run's normal PR/commit-gate to carry on the run's OWN branch (never main), or (b) sling an evolve bead (Producer A) and let the drainer open the PR. Add a guard: refuse a machinery commit whose branch is main/dev/master. Cross-check stage-reflect.md + self-evolution.md + machinery_edit.py + the commit stage.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/references/stage-reflect.md, plugins/flow/skills/flow/references/self-evolution.md, plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/machinery_edit.py
HOT FILE: True
AUDIT SOURCES: friction-history mining (live dogfood observation)
AUDIT ID: reflect-machinery-commits-to-main
close_reason: Merged via PR #60 (squash). machinery_edit.py now code-refuses apply on a protected branch (main/master/dev/develop) -> machinery self-edits can never commit to main; reflect routes to PROPOSE+RECORD/evolve-bead. Gate verified on main (seam_check green, test_machinery_edit pass).

title: Beads (bd) CLI companion skill
description: tracker_beads.py is ~28K of bd orchestration and CLAUDE.md mandates bd for all tracking, but no skill teaches the bd CLI upfront. The agent rediscovers the powerful bd interface (ready/show/create/dep/update/sync) every session. Open fork: bundle here as a flow companion (encodes flow's bd gotchas, e.g. backend-transition gap flow-7qm, in_review-on-review-less-tracker flow-970) vs the general vdsmon-skills marketplace (plain bd literacy, reusable anywhere).
close_reason: All children (hry.1 skill scaffold #55, hry.2 gotchas #56) merged. bd companion skill shipped under plugins/flow/skills/bd/. Epic complete.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: CITATION FIX (advisor/decomp): source text mislabels the gotcha IDs. flow-7qm is 'bd init invasiveness (--skip-agents --non-interactive)', flow-970 is 'gate tests/ under ty' — neither is the cited gotcha. VERIFIED gotchas the companion skill should encode: (a) bd-init invasiveness = flow-7qm; (b) backend-transition gap (ticket claim open->in_progress) = flow-fab; (c) no in_review state on a review-less tracker (beads exposes only in_progress|blocked|closed) documented firsthand in references/stage-commit.md exit-3 handling.

title: Tracker-agnostic ticket authoring front door for flow
description: Flow has no authoring path: /flow <ticket> assumes a pre-existing key. /jira-create is the de-facto front door but Jira-only. Nothing serves the beads side. Need a thin, tracker-agnostic way to sling a ticket stating the PROBLEM only (summary + short context + type), no solution/plan; planning is flow's spec stage's job. Open fork: flow verb (reuses workspace config, flow-repos only) vs standalone skill (works anywhere, own config).
close_reason: Both children closed: uo7.1 (verb-new.md, PR #57, over-delivered the SKILL.md router row + bump) and uo7.2 (verified already-satisfied no-op). /flow new authoring front door shipped. Epic complete.

title: sync._invoke comment/link/edit op branches + unknown-op fallthrough never exercised through reconcile()
description:
EVIDENCE:
sync.py:_invoke (63-79) dispatches on op in {transition, comment, link, edit} plus a final `return False`. All 3 tests in test_sync.py seed pending mutations with op='transition' only (lines 43,59,74). The fake _Tracker stubs comment/link/edit (25-31) but no test routes those through reconcile(), so 3 dispatch branches + the unknown-op guard stay unexecuted.

VALUE / RATIONALE:
The pending-mutation drain is load-bearing safety; 3 of its 4 op handlers plus the unknown-op guard are untested, so a malformed link/edit replay could throw or silently no-op uncaught. Cheap to close (fixture already exists). Distinct from the dead-enqueue finding. Touches one test file.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/tests/test_sync.py
HOT FILE: False
AUDIT SOURCES: tests-coverage mining
AUDIT ID: sync-nontransition-op-branches-untested
close_reason: Merged to main via PR #36 (squash). Beads backend not auto-transitioned by reflect (flow-7qm/fab deviation); closing manually to match shipped state.

title: iter_jsonl re-quarantines the same malformed line on every read; sidecar grows unbounded
description:
EVIDENCE:
_jsonl.py:30-52 iter_jsonl appends each bad line to the quarantine sidecar and explicitly never rewrites the main file. pending_mutations.py:133 calls iter_jsonl on every append (dedup scan) and :209 on every compact; recall paths call it on every recall. Because the offending line stays in the main file, one corrupt line in pending-mutations.jsonl or knowledge.jsonl appends one new sidecar record per call, forever, and is reprocessed each time.

VALUE / RATIONALE:
Unbounded disk growth and repeated wasted work from a single corrupt line, on a workspace that ever sees one malformed JSONL line. The append-only main-file invariant is correct and load-bearing, so the fix is dedup at the sidecar (track already-quarantined raw lines per pass, or key by content hash) — do not rewrite the source. Isolated to _jsonl.py.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/_jsonl.py
HOT FILE: False
AUDIT SOURCES: robustness-review
AUDIT ID: jsonl-quarantine-unbounded-growth

title: Atomic-write helper reimplemented 4x weaker than shared _atomicio (dropped dir-fsync + temp cleanup)
description:
EVIDENCE:
Verified: `def _atomic_write` defined in diff_extract.py, init.py, state.py, ticket_frontmatter.py — separate from the shared _atomicio.atomic_write_bytes (imported by 8 modules). Canonical does temp+fsync+os.replace plus (a) parent-dir fsync for crash-durable rename, (b) temp unlink on BaseException. The four local copies stop at os.replace with no dir-fsync and use NamedTemporaryFile(delete=False), leaking the .tmp on mid-write exception. state.py added the dir-fsync back (proving the gap was noticed once) but still leaks the temp — so the duplication has already drifted: one of four hand-patched, three not.

VALUE / RATIONALE:
Restoring these call sites to the shared helper recovers parent-dir fsync (rename durability across crash) and temp cleanup the copies dropped — aligns with the load-bearing 'atomic writes + quarantine' invariant rather than eroding it. Low real-world trigger (crash/mid-write only) but it is exactly the robustness machinery the project calls load-bearing, and the drift means future safety edits must repeat 5 places. Mechanical, reviewable.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/diff_extract.py, plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/init.py, plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/ticket_frontmatter.py, plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/state.py, plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/_atomicio.py
HOT FILE: False
AUDIT SOURCES: dead-code-complexity mining
AUDIT ID: atomic-write-reimplemented-weaker

title: No test asserts every registry reference_doc resolves to a real file; seam_check is blind to it
description:
EVIDENCE:
test_registry.py (37 lines) only checks load/by-name/malformed; never iterates entries asserting Path(reference_doc).is_file() (grep of is_file|exists|reference_doc returns only the REAL_REGISTRY path constant). validate_workspace.py has no reference_doc existence check. seam_check.py validates only ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/scripts/<x>.py invocations against argparse — it ran green (exit 0) while two registry docs are missing. So registry-missing-stage-docs is invisible to all three guards (lint, seam_check, suite).

VALUE / RATIONALE:
The regression net that would have caught the missing-docs drift at commit time. A short parametrized test over load_registry(REAL_REGISTRY) asserting (SKILL_ROOT / e.reference_doc).is_file() for every entry closes the seam_check blind spot for the registry→doc edge. Ship as the guard pairing with registry-missing-stage-docs. Touches only a test file.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/tests/test_registry.py
HOT FILE: False
AUDIT SOURCES: flow architecture-seam mining
AUDIT ID: test-registry-doc-existence
close_reason: Merged to main via PR #41 (squash). Beads backend not auto-transitioned by reflect (flow-7qm/fab deviation); closing manually to match shipped state.

title: Registry points at two reference docs that do not exist (stage-create_pr.md, stage-review_loop.md)
description:
EVIDENCE:
stage-registry.toml:128/142 set reference_doc to references/stage-create_pr.md and stage-review_loop.md; verified both MISSING (`ls references/stage-*.md` returns only code_review/commit/e2e/implement/plan/reflect/ticket; test -f confirms both absent; git log empty — never committed). dispatch_stage.py emits reference_doc unconditionally. Both default_handler=none so the bare happy path skips the Read, keeping it latent — but reconfiguring either to inline/subagent via the init wizard hits SKILL.md's 'Read ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/${descriptor.reference_doc}' on a nonexistent path and the stage breaks. e2e is also default-none yet ships stage-e2e.md for exactly this case; create_pr/review_loop do not (the asymmetry is the tell). Folds in: SKILL.md routing section (lines ~191-205) also omits create_pr/review_loop, camouflaging the dangling entries.

VALUE / RATIONALE:
Silently violates the registry's stated one-entry-one-doc contract (stage-registry.toml header). Latent today but a hard failure the moment a workspace opts into a PR-creating/review inline handler — the exact reconfigure path the registry advertises. Fix: write the two docs mirroring stage-e2e.md, or drop reference_doc from entries that genuinely never need one (and align the SKILL.md routing note). Touches stage-registry.toml/SKILL.md = hot.

title: inventory.md beads contract table documents wrong flag: `bd update --labels` but code calls `--set-labels`
description:
EVIDENCE:
inventory.md `bd update <key>` row lists `--labels`; tracker_beads.py:487 calls `_run(["update", key, "--set-labels", joined])` and its own comment (line 484) says bd update has --set-labels/--add-label. Verified both: the update row says --labels, the code uses --set-labels. (bd create DOES use --labels at tracker_beads.py:450, matching the create row — drift is specific to the update row.)

VALUE / RATIONALE:
Wrong value in the authoritative beads-CLI contract table, not a gap. Anyone (human or the reflect/self-evolution loop) consulting it to build a `bd update` labels invocation emits `--labels`, which bd rejects. One-line doc fix, ironclad evidence.

title: git status/ls-files/diff parsed without core.quotePath=false: special-char paths break the ownership gate
description:
EVIDENCE:
diff_extract.py:323 check_ownership parses `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all` via `line[3:].strip()`; :132 _untracked_files parses `git ls-files`; :153 diff_since parses `git diff --numstat` — all via splitlines, none pass core.quotePath=false (verified: grep -c quotePath diff_extract.py = 0). Reproduced in scratch repo: default git emits `?? "sub/n\303\251w.py"` (quoted, C-escaped); with `core.quotePath false` emits literal `sub/néw.py`. A planned file named `weird name.py` parses as the quoted string and never matches the owned set, flagging a legit file as unowned.

VALUE / RATIONALE:
Correctness/availability bug in the load-bearing content-ownership commit gate (stage-implement.md:36, stage-commit.md). Any planned filename with a space, non-ASCII byte, quote, or backslash gets mis-classified and can block a legit commit. Not a safety bypass (quoted string never goes permissive). Fix is one line at three call sites: `git -c core.quotePath=false ...` or `-z` + NUL split. diff_extract is safety-sensitive but not a wired stage handler.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/diff_extract.py
HOT FILE: False
AUDIT SOURCES: robustness-review
AUDIT ID: git-porcelain-quotepath-parsing

title: ty type-check omits hooks/ despite lint task description claiming coverage
description:
EVIDENCE:
mise.toml [tasks.lint] description says 'ty check (scripts + plugin hooks)' but run list is `ruff check . ../../../hooks`, `ruff format --check . ../../../hooks`, `ty check .` — the ty step is bare `ty check .` (scripts only). Verified: the two ruff steps include ../../../hooks, ty does not. CI runs `mise run lint` from the scripts dir, so hooks/session-start.py (276 lines, fully annotated) is never type-checked. Running `ty check ../../../hooks` passes today, proving it is an omission not a deliberate exclusion.

VALUE / RATIONALE:
The SessionStart recall hook is wired into every session; a future type regression there (wrong dict shape, None deref) ships green because ty never sees the file. Fix is one token (`ty check . ../../../hooks`). Poster-child candidate: verified, isolated, every-session blast, near-zero review cost.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/mise.toml
HOT FILE: False
AUDIT SOURCES: quality-gates mining
AUDIT ID: ty-check-skips-hooks

title: throwaway transition probe
init.py: pass --skip-agents --non-interactive to bd init
description: flow's init.py runs bare 'bd init --prefix <prefix>' (init.py:498). bd init then generates AGENTS.md and .claude/settings.json (a bd prime SessionStart/PreCompact hook) and auto-commits them — invasive for a shared, Claude-Code-only repo. bd has '--skip-agents' (help: 'Skip AGENTS.md and Claude settings generation') which suppresses both at the source, and '--non-interactive' for headless/background safety (flow often runs detached). Fix: add both flags to the bd init invocation in _run_bd_init. Consider --skip-hooks too (decide whether flow wants bd's export-on-commit git hooks). Surfaced while dogfooding flow on flow.
acceptance_criteria: bd init invoked with --skip-agents --non-interactive; a fresh /flow init backend=beads produces no AGENTS.md and no .claude/settings.json; existing init tests updated for the new argv.

title: Gate tests/ under the ty type checker
description: The ty type gate currently excludes tests/ (set in scripts/pyproject.toml [tool.ty.src] exclude = ["tests"]). Bring tests under the gate: add [tool.ty.analysis] allowed-unresolved-imports = ["pytest"] (32 of 52 test diagnostics are just pytest being unresolvable in ty's env), drop tests from the exclude (keep tests/_fixtures excluded), then triage and fix the ~20 real test type diagnostics. Success: mise run lint passes with tests included, and a deliberately-broken annotation in a test file makes the gate exit 1.
acceptance_criteria: mise run lint green with tests type-checked; broken annotation in a test file fails the gate; full pytest suite still passes.

title: ops deadman (shipped #264) renders only inside flow workspace; weigh unconditional machine-wide render
description: FINDING (flow-fd88 superseded-arm work, NOT shipped): #264's SessionStart deadman (staleness_block + jsonl run-record) is gated behind the workspace check — cli_main early-returns 0 when find_workspace_root is None, so the 'nightly/weekly loop stale' warning only fires in sessions started inside the flow repo. The armed evolve schedule is machine-level (~/.flow-evolve/), so a maintainer working in ANOTHER repo never sees the stale-loop warning — partially defeating the deadman's machine-wide purpose. CANDIDATE FIX (green prototype exists in the abandoned flow-fd88 worktree diff): hoist the deadman block above the workspace gate and add a run_record_path DI kwarg to cli_main so it renders first + unconditionally; absence of the record file keeps it silent on unarmed machines. TRADEOFF the maintainer must rule on: unconditional => the warning surfaces in EVERY session across ALL repos on the machine (cross-project noise) vs the current flow-repo-only scope. #264 was the 10/10 wave-4 winner and shipped workspace-gated deliberately or incidentally — unclear which. Low priority; decide before implementing.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: (advisor) Hoist the evolve-loop deadman (staleness_block) above the workspace gate in cli_main so it renders machine-wide, not only inside flow-repo sessions. Rationale: staleness_block already returns "" on an absent record (unarmed machine) and on all-healthy schedules, so it emits ONLY when a schedule is armed AND genuinely stale/failed/hung — the 'cross-project noise' the ticket weighs is gated by a real fault. The armed schedule lives at ~/.flow-evolve/ (machine-level), so flow-repo-only scope partially defeats the deadman's machine-wide purpose. Safe to auto-ship: is_hot=false, blast radius is one hook function (warning-only render), trivially reversible, CI-covered by an added positive test. Maintainer retains the final ruling at PR review — the deliverable is a draft PR, and the PR description foregrounds the cross-project-visibility tradeoff so it is exercised at merge. Implementation: add an optional run_record_path DI kwarg to cli_main (test-isolation per flow-cfj8) and restructure the workspace_root-is-None early-return so staleness renders unconditionally while build_context stays workspace-gated.

title: Encode flow's verified bd gotchas into the companion skill
description: Add a 'flow's bd gotchas' section to the bd companion skill (per the corrected citations on flow-hry): (a) bd-init invasiveness -> use --skip-agents --non-interactive (flow-7qm); (b) backend-transition gap: ticket stage claims open->in_progress (flow-fab); (c) no in_review on a review-less tracker (beads exposes only in_progress|blocked|closed) -> commit step must NOT auto-close via done, documented in references/stage-commit.md exit-3. Acceptance: all three gotchas present with the concrete flag/state-set + the don't-auto-close rule. Depends on flow-hry.

title: Add a bd companion skill teaching the core bd CLI surface
description: Add a bd companion skill under plugins/flow/skills/bd/SKILL.md (auto-discovered via plugin.json skills=./skills, no marketplace wiring). Teaches the bd surface the agent rediscovers each session: ready/show/create/dep/update/sync, plus the local-Dolt + refs/dolt/data sync model and the .beads/issues.jsonl passive-export caveat. Lean, trigger-scoped to bd usage. Acceptance: skill loads, lists the six verbs w/ one-line usage, states the sync architecture. PLACEMENT: defaulted to in-flow-plugin (zero cross-marketplace blast) per advisor; sibling-plugin alternative is the open fork left to the user.

title: Tracker-agnostic ship/merge tail past draft PR
description: Flow stops at draft-PR-for-human-review; it owns create_pr + review_loop but not the mark-ready -> merge step. /ship-it covers this but is Bitbucket+bkt+CodeRabbit-locked and lives in another marketplace. Gap: a backend/VCS-agnostic 'end' for flow. Lowest priority; only if flow should own past draft PR. Extend verb-do/stage-commit, do not clone /ship-it.
close_reason: Won't-do (maintainer decision). Evolve beads ALREADY auto-merge via the drainer (evolve_reap.py green-leaf reap + kos's hot-in-isolation auto_merge_hot). 0gb's scope was a GENERAL merge tail for non-evolve /flow runs (any Jira/beads ticket merging itself past the draft PR); maintainer wants the human-merge-the-PR gate to stay for normal runs. Keep auto-merge evolve-only. Decomposition (merge.py gh-handler + registered merge stage) shelved.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DEFERRED pending a product decision (advisor-flagged): the bead's own text says 'lowest priority; only if flow should own past draft PR'. Building it injects a new stage-registry.toml 'merge' entry (HOT, canonical stage vocab) for an unresolved 'should flow own the merge tail?' question. Decomposition is ready (merge.py gh-handler + registered merge stage after review_loop, NOT stage-commit) and will be filed on a 'build 0gb' go-ahead. Open sub-question: terminal beads state post-merge (close vs leave for human).

title: No coverage instrumentation in dev tooling — untested-public-method gaps are invisible to CI
description:
EVIDENCE:
`import coverage` and `import pytest_cov` both fail (ModuleNotFoundError) in system python3 AND the mise dev venv; mise.toml `mise run test` runs plain pytest with no --cov. The three method-level gaps (jira writes, sync branches, beads set_description) pass CI green at 826 tests because nothing measures executed lines — they were only found by manual method-vs-test grep.

VALUE / RATIONALE:
Adding pytest-cov as a dev dep + a `mise run cov` task (or a CI floor) makes exactly this class of untested-public-method drift visible automatically instead of relying on manual audits. Meta/tooling — enables the other test-gap tickets but lower direct value. Touches dev config only.

title: atomic_write_text silently narrows file permissions to 0o600 on every rewrite
description:
EVIDENCE:
_atomicio.py:18 uses tempfile.mkstemp (creates at 0o600) then os.replace, which keeps the temp mode and discards the destination's prior mode. Verified empirically by the miner: a 0o664 file becomes 0o600 after one call. Every safety rewrite flows through here: run.lock (lease.py:391), state.json, snapshot.json (snapshot.py:192-194), pending-mutations.jsonl (pending_mutations.py:215).

VALUE / RATIONALE:
Harmless for single-user workspaces but in a group-/CI-shared workspace a previously group-readable state/lease file becomes owner-only after the first rewrite, breaking a recover/status invoked under another account. Fix: capture destination st_mode before os.replace and re-chmod (fall back 0o644 when absent), the standard atomic-write pattern. Isolated to _atomicio.py.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/_atomicio.py
HOT FILE: False
AUDIT SOURCES: robustness-review
AUDIT ID: atomic-write-narrows-perms
close_reason: Merged to main via PR #37 (squash). Beads backend not auto-transitioned by reflect (flow-7qm/fab deviation); closing manually to match shipped state.

title: Runner type alias + _default_runner factory duplicated across 7 modules with two incompatible callable shapes under one name
description:
EVIDENCE:
Runner = Callable[..., CompletedProcess[str]] redefined identically in tracker_beads/diff_extract/branch_ticket/init/recall_pending; flow_worktree.py:47 uses the STRICTER Callable[[list[str], Path], CompletedProcess[str]] under the same name; lease.py:111 binds the same names to a different shape run(args:list[str])->str (check=True, no cwd). _default_runner reimplemented byte-identically in 6 modules. _quarantine_path is a 2-line dup (recall_pending.py:87 vs pending_mutations.py:71).

VALUE / RATIONALE:
Useful signal is the drift: 'Runner' names two incompatible contracts, so a naive 'consolidate to one shared Runner' would break lease.py's typing or loosen flow_worktree's. The 5-6 identical (list[str],Path) copies are safe to hoist; lease.py must stay separate. Worth a ticket so a future consolidation does not blindly merge them. Low stakes.

title: _parse_iso reimplemented 5 times under one name with three different contracts
description:
EVIDENCE:
Verified: `def _parse_iso` in heartbeat.py, recall_pending.py, lease.py (byte-identical bare fromisoformat), metric.py (adds .replace('Z','+00:00') + AttributeError catch), validate_postmortem.py (OPPOSITE contract — REQUIRES trailing Z, returns None otherwise). All parse engine-written timestamps and every writer emits a trailing Z. On pinned py3.12 bare fromisoformat accepts 'Z' so no live bug, but metric.py's .replace is dead defensive code and validate_postmortem's Z-required branch is a divergent contract.

VALUE / RATIONALE:
Latent maintenance trap, not a current bug: same name, three semantics. A shared helper collapses five copies to one and makes the Z-handling contract explicit and testable once. Low stakes, mechanical.

title: No gate enforces MODULE.md completeness vs scripts on disk
description:
EVIDENCE:
The repo gates the prose↔CLI seam (seam_check.py + test_seam_check.py) but nothing checks that every engine script appears in MODULE.md. The heartbeat.py omission is caught by a one-line `comm -23 <(ls *.py|grep -v ^test) <(grep -oE '[a-z_]+\.py' MODULE.md)` yet no test or CI runs it. seam_check validates invocation flags, not doc-map coverage.

VALUE / RATIONALE:
First instance (heartbeat) already shipped; without a guard MODULE.md drifts every time a script is added without a manual table edit. A trivial test (set of *.py minus test_*.py ⊆ names in MODULE.md) turns silent navigation drift into a CI failure. Pairs as the prevention for module-md-refresh. Touches test/check files.

title: MODULE.md drifted: missing heartbeat.py row + omits download-attachments / check-ownership subcommands + stale flag summaries
description:
EVIDENCE:
Four instances of one drift, all verified. (1) heartbeat.py (11.2K, wired by stage-registry/_registry/state/recover) has 0 references in MODULE.md (grep -c = 0) — the only engine .py absent from the map. (2) tracker_cli row omits `download-attachments` (actual --help set includes it). (3) diff_extract row omits `check-ownership` — the content-ownership commit gate, corroborated by dev-history.md:14 and SKILL.md; a safety subcommand invisible in the map. (4) flow_friction omits --severity, flow_worktree create omits --worktree-path/--copy/--no-mise-trust, resolve_handler omits --search-roots (MODULE.md:3 scopes to 'key flags', so flag omissions are softer than the missing-row/missing-subcommand cases).

VALUE / RATIONALE:
MODULE.md is the CLAUDE.md-designated 'read this to find a script' map for ~24k LOC; a wired stage module and two whole subcommands (one a load-bearing safety gate) being invisible defeats it. Ship as one consolidated refresh pass, not four tickets. Pairs with module-md-completeness-guard. Touches one doc.
